string;\n21\t}\n22\t\n23\tinterface ValidationErrorResponse {\n24\t  error: \"validation failed\";\n25\t  details: ValidationErrorDetail[];\n26\t}\n27\t\n28\tfunction formatZodError(error: ZodError): ValidationErrorDetail[] {\n29\t  return error.issues.map((issue) => {\n30\t    // For nested objects, join the path with dots\n31\t    const field = issue.path.map((p) => (typeof p === \"number\" ? `[${p}]` : p)).join(\".\");\n32\t\n33\t    if (issue.code === ZodIssueCode.invalid_enum_value) {\n34\t      return {\n35\t        field,\n36\t        message: `Must be one of: ${issue.options.join(\", \")}`,\n37\t      };\n38\t    }\n39\t\n40\t    if (issue.code === ZodIssueCode.too_small) {\n41\t      if (issue.type === \"string\") {\n42\t        return {\n43\t          field,\n44\t          message: issue.message || `String must be at least ${issue.minimum} character(s)`,\n45\t        };\n46\t      }\n47\t      if (issue.type === \"array\") {\n48\t        return {\n49\t          field,\n50\t          message: issue.message || `Array must contain at least ${issue.minimum} item(s)`,\n51\t        };\n52\t      }\n53\t      if (issue.type === \"number\") {\n54\t        return { field, message: issue.message || `Number must be at least ${issue.minimum}` };\n55\t      }\n56\t    }\n57\t\n58\t    if (issue.code === ZodIssueCode.too_big) {\n59\t      if (issue.type === \"string\") {\n60\t        return {\n61\t          field,\n62\t          message: issue.message || `String must be at most ${issue.maximum} character(s)`,\n63\t        };\n64\t      }\n65\t      if (issue.type === \"array\") {\n66\t        return {\n67\t          field,\n68\t          message: issue.message || `Array must contain at most ${issue.maximum} item(s)`,\n69\t        };\n70\t      }\n71\t      if (issue.type === \"number\") {\n72\t        return { field, message: issue.message || `Number must be at most ${issue.maximum}` };\n73\t      }\n74\t    }\n75\t\n76\t    return { field, message: issue.message || `Invalid value` };\n77\t  });\n78\t}\n79\t\n80\t/**\n81\t * Parse and validate a JSON request body against a Zod schema.\n82\t * Returns the validated data on success, or a Response on failure.\n83\t * Caller must check `if (body instanceof Response) return body;`\n84\t *\n85\t * Sanitizes all string inputs to prevent XSS, SQL injection, and other attacks.\n86\t */\n87\texport async function validateBody<T>(c: Context, schema: ZodSchema<T>): Promise<T | Response> {\n88\t  let body: unknown;\n89\t  try {\n90\t    body = await c.req.json();\n91\t  } catch {\n92\t    const errorResponse: ValidationErrorResponse = {\n93\t      error: \"validation failed\",\n94\t      details: [{ field: \"\", message: \"Request body must be valid JSON\" }],\n95\t    };\n96\t    logger.warn(\"Request body validation failed: invalid JSON\", {\n97\t      path: c.req.path,\n98\t      method: c.req.method,\n99\t    });\n100\t    return c.json(errorResponse, 400);\n101\t  }\n102\t\n103\t  // Sanitize string fields in the body before validation\n104\t  // This prevents XSS, SQL injection, and other injection attacks\n105\t  body = sanitizeObject(body);\n106\t\n107\t  const result = schema.safeParse(body);\n108\t\n109\t  if (!result.success) {\n110\t    const errorResponse: ValidationErrorResponse = {\n111\t      error: \"validation failed\",\n112\t      details: formatZodError(result.error),\n113\t    };\n114\t    logger.warn(\"Request body validation failed\", {\n115\t      path: c.req.path,\n116\t      method: c.req.method,\n117\t      errors: formatZodError(result.error),\n118\t    });\n119\t    return c.json(errorResponse, 400);\n120\t  }\n121\t\n122\t  return result.data;\n123\t}\n124\t\n125\t/**\n126\t * Sanitize path parameters to prevent injection attacks.\n127\t *\n128\t * Path parameters can be exploited for:\n129\t * - Path traversal attacks (../../)\n130\t * - Command injection\n131\t * - XSS via reflected parameters\n132\t *\n133\t * This function sanitizes path parameter values before they reach\n134\t * the route handler, providing defense in depth.\n135\t */\n136\tfunction sanitizePathParams(params: Record<string, string>): Record<string, string> {\n137\t  const sanitized: Record<string, string> = {};\n138\t  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(params)) {\n139\t    // Sanitize the key as well\n140\t    const sanitizedKey = sanitizeStringSimple(key, {\n141\t      normalizeWhitespace: false,\n142\t      maxLength: 100,\n143\t    });\n144\t\n145\t    // Sanitize the value - focus on path traversal and command injection\n146\t    // for path params since they're typically IDs or enum values\n147\t    sanitized[sanitizedKey] = sanitizeStringSimple(value, {\n148\t      stripHtml: true,\n149\t      preventSqlInjection: true,\n150\t      preventCommandInjection: true,\n151\t      preventPathTraversal: true,\n152\t      normalizeWhitespace: false, // against a Zod schema.\n161\t * Returns the validated data on success, or a Response on failure.\n162\t * Caller must check `if (query instanceof Response) return query;`\n163\t *\n164\t * Uses sanitized query parameters from the inputSanitization middleware\n165\t * if available, providing defense in depth against injection attacks.\n166\t */\n167\texport function validateQuery<T>(c: Context, schema: ZodSchema<T>): T | Response {\n168\t  // Prefer sanitized query parameters from the inputSanitization middleware\n169\t  // This provides defense in depth - sanitization happens before validation\n170\t  const sanitizedQuery = (c.get(\"sanitizedQuery\") as Record<string, string>) || undefined;\n171\t\n172\t  // Fall back to raw query params if sanitization hasn't run\n173\t  const queryParams = sanitizedQuery || c.req.query();\n174\t\n175\t  const result = schema.safeParse(queryParams);\n176\t  if (!result.success) {\n177\t    const errorResponse: ValidationErrorResponse c.req.path,\n183\t      method: c.req.method,\n184\t      errors: formatZodError(result.error),\n185\t    });\n186\t    return c.json(errorResponse, 400);\n187\t  }\n188\t  return result.data;\n189\t}\n190\t\n191\t/**\n192\t * Validate path parameters against a Zod schema.\n193\t * Returns the validated data on success, or a Response on failure.\n194\t * Caller must check `if (params instanceof Response) return params;`\n195\t *\n196\t * Sanitizes path parameters to prevent injection attacks including\n197\t * path traversal, command injection, and reflected XSS.\n198\t */\n199\texport function validateParams<T>(c: Context, schema: ZodSchema<T>): T | Response {\n200\t  const pathParams = c.req.param();\n201\t\n202\t  // Sanitize path parameters before \"/nonexistent/dist\");\n404\t}\n405\t\n406\t/**\n407\t * Helper to get a CSRF token from the test app.\n408\t * Makes a GET request to /api/csrf-token and extracts the token.\n409\t */\n410\tasync function getCsrfToken(app: Hono): Promise<string> {\n411\t  const res = await app.request(\"/api/csrf-token\");\n412\t  expect(res.status).toBe(200);\n413\t  const body = await res.json();\n414\t  return body.token as string;\n415\t}\n416\t\n417\t/**\n418\t * Helper to make a state-changing request with CSRF token.\n419\t * Gets a fresh token and includes it in the X-CSRF-Token header.\n420\t */\n421\tasync function _requestWithCsrf(\n422\t  app: Hono,\n423\t  path: string,\n424\t  options: RequestInit = {}\n425\t): Promise<Response> {\n426\t  const token = await getCsrfToken(app);\n427\t  return app.request(path, {\n428\t    ...options,\n429\t    headers: {\n430\t      ...options.headers,\n431\t      \"X-CSRF-Token\": token,\n432\t    },\n433\t  });\n434\t}\n435\t\n436\t/**\n437\t * Helper to make an authenticated state-changing request with CSRF token.\n438\t * Combines API key authentication with CSRF protection.\n439\t */\n440\tasync function requestWithAuthAndCsrf(\n441\t  app: Hono,\n442\t  path: string,\n443\t  options: RequestInit = {}\n444\t): Promise<Response> {\n445\t  const credentials = await createTestUserCredentials();\n446\t  const token = await getCsrfToken(app);\n447\t  return app.request(path, {\n448\t    ...options,\n449\t    headers: {\n450\t      Authorization: credentials.authorizationHeader,\n451\t      ...options.headers,\n452\t      \"X-CSRF-Token\": token,\n453\t    },\n454\t  });\n455\t}\n456\t\n457\t/**\n458\t * Helper to make an authenticated admin request with CSRF token.\n459\t * Uses admin credentials for operations that require elevated privileges.\n460\t */\n461\tasync function requestWithAdminAndCsrf(\n462\t  app: Hono,\n463\t  path: string,\n464\t  options: RequestInit = {}\n465\t): Promise<Response> {\n466\t Supports brotli, gzip, and deflate based on Accept-Encoding header.\n288\t * Brotli is preferred for best compression ratio.\n289\t */\n290\tfunction compressionMiddleware(): MiddlewareHandler {\n291\t  return async (c, next) => {\n292\t    await next();\n293\t\n294\t    const body = c.res.body;\n295\t    if (!body || c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Encoding\")) return;\n296\t\n297\t    const c.res.clone().arrayBuffer());\n308\t\n309\t    // Prefer brotli for best compression, then gzip, then deflate\n310\t    if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"br\")) {\n311\t      const brotlied = await new chunks.push(chunk));\n315\t        brotli.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n316\t        brotli.on(\"error\", reject);\n317\t        brotli.end(buffer);\n318\t      });\n319\t\n320\t      c.res = new Response(brotlied, {\n321\t        status: c.res.status,\n322\t        headers: c.res.headers,\n323\t      });\n324\t      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"br\");\n325\t chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n329\t        const gzip = createGzip();\n330\t        gzip.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n331\t        gzip.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n332\t        gzip.on(\"error\", reject);\n333\t        gzip.end(buffer);\n334\t      });\n335\t\n336\t      c.res = new Response(gzipped, {\n337\t        status: createDeflate();\n346\t        deflate.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n347\t        deflate.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n348\t        deflate.on(\"error\", });\n356\t      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"deflate\");\n357\t      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n358\t    }\n359\t  };\n360\t}\n361\t\n362\t/**\n363\t * Check if a path has httpResponseSplitting());\n408\t\n409\t  // Host header protection to prevent cache poisoning and password reset poisoning\n410\t  // In production, set ALLOWED_HOSTS environment variable to restrict allowed hosts\n411\t  const isProduction = process.env[\"NODE_ENV\"] === \"production\";\n412\t  const allowedHosts = process.env[\"ALLOWED_HOSTS\"]\n413\t    ? process.env[\"ALLOWED_HOSTS\"].split(\",\")\n414\t    : undefined;\n415\t  app.use(\n416\t    \"*\",\n417\t    hostHeaderProtection({\n418\t      allowedHosts,\n419\t      blockMissingHost: isProduction, // Only require Host header in production\n420\t      blockIpAddresses: isProduction,\n421\t      blockPrivateNetworks: isProduction,\n422\t      blockLocalhost: isProduction, // Block localhost requests in production\n423\t    })\n424\t  );\n425\t\n426\t  // Distributed tracing for all requests\n427\t  app.use(\"*\", tracingMiddleware);\n428\t\n429\t  // Request size limits for all routes (DoS protection)\n430\t  app.use(\"*\", requestSizeLimits());\n431\t\n432\t  // Input sanitization for all API routes (XSS, SQL injection prevention)\n433\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", inputSanitization());\n434\t\n435\t  // JSON depth protection for API routes (OWASP A01, A03)\n436\t  // Prevents DoS via deeply nested JSON structures\n437\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", jsonDepthProtection());\n438\t\n439\t  // Optional authentication for all API routes\n440\t  // Parses Authorization header and sets auth context if present\n441\t  // Individual routes can require specific permissions using requirePermission\n442\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", optionalAuth({ allowSessions: true }));\n443\t\n444\t  // CSRF protection for state-changing operations\n445\t  // Excludes health, metrics, and safe read-only endpoints\n446\t  // NOTE: /status is a public read-only HTML page (not under /api/*, so not affected)\n447\t  // NOTE: /api/context, /api/auth/oauth, /api/auth/mfa, /api/auth/session are disabled\n448\t  // NOTE: /api/auth/password is enabled for password reset functionality\n449\t  app.use(\n450\t    \"/api/*\",\n451\t    csrfProtection({\n452\t      excludePaths: [\n453\t        \"/api/health\",\n454\t        \"/api/metrics\",\n455\t        \"/api/stations\",\n456\t        \"/api/routes\",\n457\t        \"/api/static\",\n458\t        \"/api/arrivals\",\n459\t        \"/api/alerts\",\n460\t        \"/api/equipment\",\n461\t        \"/api/trip\",\n462\t        \"/api/positions\",\n463\t        \"/api/push/vapid-public-key\",\n464\t        \"/api/journal\",\n465\t        // \"/api/context\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n466\t        // \"/api/auth/oauth\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n467\t        // \"/api/auth/mfa\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n468\t        // \"/api/auth/session\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n469\t        \"/api/auth/password\",\n470\t        \"/api/csrf-token\",\n471\t        \"/api/security/csp-report\", // CSP violation reports (browser-initiated)\n472\t      ],\n473\t    })\n474\t  );\n475\t\n476\t  // HPP protection for all API routes (prevents parameter pollution attacks)\n477\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", hppProtection({ strategy: \"first\" }));\n478\t\n479\t  // HTTP metrics collection for all API routes\n480\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", httpMetrics());\n481\t\n482\t  // Rate limiting on all API routes (60 req/min per IP, token bucket)\n483\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", rateLimiter());\n484\t\n485\t  // Response size limits for API routes (OWASP A01, A04)\n486\t  // Prevents DoS via large response payloads\n487\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", responseSizeLimits());\n488\t\n489\t  // Compression for API responses\n490\t  app.use(\"/api/*\", compressionMiddleware());\n491\t\n492\t  const stationToComplex = buildStationToComplexMap(complexes);\n493\t\n494\t  // Create transfer engine for commute analysis\n495\t  const transferEngine = createTransferEngine({\n496\t    stations,\n497\t    routes,\n498\t    transfers,\n499\t    complexes,\n500\t    getArrivals: (stationId: string) => {\n501\t      return getArrivals(stationId);\n502\t    },\n503\t  });\n504\t\n505\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n506\t  // CSRF token endpoint\n507\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n508\t  app.get(\n509\t    \"/api/csrf-token\",\n510\t    cors({\n511\t      allowedOrigins: [\"*\"], // Allow all origins for CSRF token\n512\t      allowedMethods: [\"GET\"],\n513\t      allowedHeaders: [\"Content-Type\", \"Authorization\"],\n514\t    }),\n515\t    (c) => {\n516\t      const token = generateCsrfToken();\n517\t\n518\t      return c.json({ token });\n519\t    }\n520\t  );\n521\t\n522\t  /**\n523\t   * Format a timestamp as \"X ago\" for human-readable display\n524\t   */\n525\t  function timeAgo(isoString: string | null): string {\n526\t    if (!isoString) return \"Never\";\n527\t    const ms = Date.now() - new Date(isoString).getTime();\n528\t    const seconds = Math.floor(ms / 1000);\n529\t    if (seconds < 60) return `${seconds}s ago`;\n530\t    const minutes = Math.floor(seconds / 60);\n531\t    if (minutes < 60) return `${minutes}m ago`;\n532\t    const hours = Math.floor(minutes / 60);\n533\t    if (hours < 24) return `${hours}h ago`;\n534\t    const days = Math.floor(hours / 24);\n535\t    return `${days}d ago`;\n536\t  }\n537\t\n538\t  /**\n539\t   * Format bytes as human-readable (e.g., \"45.2 MB\")\n540\t   */\n541\t  function formatBytes(bytes: number): string {\n542\t    const units = [\"B\", \"KB\", \"MB\", \"GB\"];\n543\t    let i = 0;\n544\t    while (bytes >= 1024 && i < units.length - 1) {\n545\t      bytes /= 1024;\n546\t      i++;\n547\t    }\n548\t    return `${bytes.toFixed(1)} ${units[i]}`;\n549\t  }\n550\t\n551\t  /**\n552\t   * Build the HTML response for the /status dashboard\n553\t   */\n554\t  function buildStatusHtml(healthData: {\n555\t    status: string;\n556\t    timestamp: string;\n557\t    uptime_seconds: number;\n558\t    feeds: Array<{\n559\t      id: string;\n560\t      name: string;\n561\t      status: string;\n562\t      lastSuccessAt: string | null;\n563\t      lastPollAt: string }>;\n570\t    alerts: {\n571\t      count: number;\n572\t      lastSuccessAt: string | null;\n573\t      matchRate: number;\n574\t      consecutiveFailures: number;\n575\t      circuitOpen: boolean;\n576\t      unmatchedCount: number;\n577\t    };\n578\t    delayDetector: {\n579\t      enabled: boolean;\n580\t      activePredictions: number;\n581\t      lastRunAt: string | null;\n582\t    };\n583\t    delayPredictor: {\n584\t      enabled: boolean;\n585\t      modelAccuracy: number;\n586\t      lastTrainedAt: string | null;\n587\t    };\n588\t    equipment: {\n589\t      lastSuccessAt: string | null;\n590\t      outages: number;\n591\t      consecutiveFailures: number;\n592\t    };\n593\t    pushSubscriptions: number;\n594\t    cacheHitRate: number;\n595\t    memory: {\n596\t      rssBytes: number;\n597\t      heapUsedBytes: number;\n598\t      heapTotalBytes: number;\n599\t      externalBytes: number;\n600\t    };\n601\t    failingFeedsCount: number;\n602\t  }): string {\n603\t    const uptimeHours = Math.floor(healthData.uptime_seconds / 3600);\n604\t    const uptimeMinutes = Math.floor((healthData.uptime_seconds % 3600) / 60);\n605\t    const statusColor =\n606\t      healthData.status === \"ok\"\n607\t        ? \"#22c55e\"\n608\t        : healthData.failingFeedsCount >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD\n609\t          ? \"#ef4444\"\n610\t          : \"#f59e0b\";\n611\t    const statusIcon =\n612\t      healthData.status === \"ok\"\n613\t        ? \"✓\"\n614\t        : healthData.failingFeedsCount >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD\n615\t          ? \"✗\"\n616\t          : \"⚠\";\n617\t\n618\t    return `<!DOCTYPE html>\n619\t<html lang=\"en\">\n620\t<head>\n621\t  <meta charset=\"UTF-8\">\n622\t  <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\">\n623\t  <title>MTA My Way - System Status</title>\n624\t  <style>\n625\t    * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }\n626\t    body {\n627\t      font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, \"Segoe UI\", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;\n628\t      background: #0f172a;\n629\t      color: #e2e8f0;\n630\t      line-height: 1.5;\n631\t      padding: 20px;\n632\t    }\n633\t    .container { max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto; }\n634\t    h1 { font-size: 24px; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 8px; }\n635\t    .subtitle { color: #94a3b8; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 24px; }\n636\t    .status-banner {\n637\t      display: inline-flex;\n638\t      align-items: center;\n639\t      gap: 8px;\n640\t      padding: 12px 20px;\n641\t      border-radius: 8px;\n642\t      font-weight: 600;\n643\t      font-size: 16px;\n644\t      margin-bottom: 24px;\n645\t      background: ${statusColor}20;\n646\t      border: 1px solid ${statusColor}40;\n647\t    }\n648\t    .status-icon { font-size: 20px; }\n649\t    .status-text { color: ${statusColor}; }\n650\t    .grid { display: grid; gap: 20px; }\n651\t    .card {\n652\t      background: #1e293b;\n653\t      border-radius: 12px;\n654\t      padding: 20px;\n655\t      border: 1px solid #334155;\n656\t    }\n657\t    .card-title {\n658\t      font-size: 16px;\n659\t border-radius: 12px;\n693\t      font-size: 12px;\n694\t      font-weight: 500;\n695\t    }\n696\t    .status-ok { background: #22c55e20; color: #22c55e; }\n697\t    .status-stale { background: #f59e0b20; color: #f59e0b; }\n698\t    .status-circuit_open { background: #ef444420; color: #ef4444; }\n699\t    .status-never_polled { background: #64748b20; color: #94a3b8; }\n700\t    .last-updated </style>\n713\t</head>\n714\t<body>\n715\t  <div class=\"container\">\n716\t    <h1>MTA My Way</h1>\n717\t    <p class=\"subtitle\">Real-time subway data system status</p>\n718\t\n719\t    <div  <div class=\"grid\">\n725\t      <!-- System Overview -->\n726\t      <div class=\"card\">\n727\t        <div class=\"card-title\">System Overview</div>\n728\t        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n729\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n730\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">Uptime</div>\n731\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">${uptimeHours}h Subscriptions</div>\n739\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">${healthData.pushSubscriptions}</div>\n740\t          </div>\n741\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n742\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">Failing Feeds</div>\n743\t            <div class=\"metric-value\" style=\"color: ${healthData.failingFeedsCount > 0 ? \"#ef4444\" : \"#22c55e\"}\">${healthData.failingFeedsCount}</div>\n744\t          </div>\n745\t        </div>\n746\t      </div>\n747\t\n748\t      <!-- Memory Usage -->\n749\t      <div class=\"card\">\n750\t        <div class=\"card-title\">Memory Usage</div>\n751\t        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n752\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n753\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">RSS (Total)</div>\n754\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">${formatBytes(healthData.memory.rssBytes)}</div>\n755\t          </div>\n756\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n757\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">Heap class=\"metric-value\">${formatBytes(healthData.memory.externalBytes)}</div>\n764\t          </div>\n765\t        </div>\n766\t      </div>\n767\t\n768\t      <!-- GTFS-RT Feeds -->\n769\t      <div class=\"card\">\n770\t        <div class=\"card-title\">MTA Feeds (GTFS-RT)</div>\n771\t        <table class=\"feed-table\">\n772\t          <thead>\n773\t            <tr>\n774\t              <th>Feed</th>\n775\t              <th>Status</th>\n776\t              <th>Last Success</th>\n777\t              <th>Entities</th>\n778\t              <th>Latency</th>\n779\t              <th>Errors (24h)</th>\n780\t            </tr>\n781\t          </thead>\n782\t          <tbody>\n783\t            ${healthData.feeds\n784\t              .map(\n785\t                (feed) => `\n786\t            <tr>\n787\t              <td>${feed.name}</td>\n788\t              <td><span class=\"status-badge status-${feed.status}\">${feed.status.replace(/_/g, \" \")}</span></td>\n789\t              <td>${timeAgo(feed.lastSuccessAt)}</td>\n790\t              <td>${feed.entityCount}</td>\n791\t              <td>${feed.avgLatencyMs}ms</td>\n792\t              <td>${feed.errorCount24h}</td>\n793\t            </tr>\n794\t          `\n795\t              )\n796\t              .join(\"\")}\n797\t          </tbody>\n798\t        </table>\n799\t      </div>\n800\t\n801\t      <!-- Alerts Status <div class=\"metric-label\">Active Alerts</div>\n807\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">${healthData.alerts.count}</div>\n808\t          </div>\n809\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n810\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">Match Rate</div>\n811\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">${(healthData.alerts.matchRate * 100).toFixed(0)}%</div>\n812\t            <div class=\"metric-sub\">${healthData.alerts.unmatchedCount} unmatched</div>\n813\t          </div>\n814\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n815\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">Last Update</div>\n816\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">${timeAgo(healthData.alerts.lastSuccessAt)}</div>\n817\t          </div>\n818\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n819\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">Status</div>\n820\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">\n821\t              <span class=\"status-badge status-${healthData.alerts.circuitOpen ? \"circuit_open\" : \"ok\"}\">\n822\t                ${healthData.alerts.circuitOpen ? \"Circuit Open\" : \"OK\"}\n823\t              </span>\n824\t            </div>\n825\t          </div>\n826\t        </div>\n827\t      </div>\n828\t\n829\t      <!-- Delay Detection -->\n830\t      <div class=\"card\">\n831\t        <div class=\"card-title\">Delay Detection</div>\n832\t        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n833\t       </div>\n840\t            <div class=\"metric-sub\">${healthData.delayDetector.activePredictions} active predictions</div>\n841\t          </div>\n842\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n843\t    </div>\n856\t        </div>\n857\t      </div>\n858\t    </div>\n859\t\n860\t    <div class=\"last-updated\">\n861\t      Last updated: ${new Date(healthData.timestamp).toLocaleString()} •\n862\t      <a href=\"/api/health\" style=\"color: #38bdf8;\">View JSON</a>\n863\t    </div>\n864\t  </div>\n865\t</body>\n866\t</html>`;\n867\t  }\n868\t\n869\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n870\t  // Health endpoint\n871\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n872\t  /**\n873\t   * GET /api/health — Per-feed health status (JSON)\n874\t   *\n875\t   * Query validation: Uses `emptyQuerySchema` (`z.object({}).strict()`).\n876\t   * Any query parameters at all cause a 400 with `{ error: \"validation failed\", details: [...] }`.\n877\t   * No query parameters are accepted.\n878\t   *\n879\t   * Response top-level fields:\n880\t   *   status              (\"ok\" | \"degraded\")\n881\t   *     — \"ok\" when all feeds have circuitOpenAt===null AND lastSuccessAt!==null AND !isStale,\n882\t   *       AND alerts has circuitOpen===false AND lastSuccessAt!==null.\n883\t   *     — \"degraded\" otherwise.\n884\t   *\n885\t   *   timestamp           string (ISO 8601) — new Date().toISOString() at request time.\n886\t   *\n887\t   *   uptime_seconds      number — seconds since SERVER_START_MS (set at module load).\n888\t   *\n889\t   *   feeds               Array<FeedState> — one entry per GTFS-realtime feed.\n890\t   *     Each element:\n891\t   *       id                    string   — feed identifier (e.g. \"1\", \"26\", \"31\", \"36\").\n892\t   *       name                  string   — human-readable feed name.\n893\t   *       status                (\"circuit_open\" | \"never_polled\" | \"stale\" | \"ok\")\n894\t   *         — \"circuit_open\" when circuitOpenAt !== null.\n895\t   *         — \"never_polled\" when lastSuccessAt === null.\n896\t   *         — \"stale\" when isStale === true.\n897\t   *         — \"ok\" otherwise.\n898\t   *       lastSuccessAt         string | null — ISO 8601 of last successful poll, or null.\n899\t   *       lastPollAt            string | null — ISO 8601 of last poll attempt, or null.\n900\t   *       consecutiveFailures  number — count of consecutive failed polls.\n901\t   *       entityCount           number — number of entities in the feed.\n902\t   *       lastError             string | null — truncated (max 100 chars) last error message,\n903\t   *                                  newlines stripped; null if no error.\n904\t   *       tripReplacementPeriod string | null — the feed's trip replacement period.\n905\t   *       avgLatencyMs          number — average of latencyHistory entries.\n906\t   *       errorCount24h         number — count of error timestamps within the last 24 hours.\n907\t   *       parseErrors           (feed-specific type) — parse error details for the feed.\n908\t   *\n909\t   *   alerts              object — alerts polling subsystem status.\n910\t   *     count                  number  — number of alerts tracked.\n911\t   *     lastSuccessAt          number | null — epoch-ms of last successful alert fetch, or null.\n912\t   *     matchRate              number  — fraction of alerts that matched trips (0–1).\n913\t   *     consecutiveFailures    number  — consecutive alert fetch failures.\n914\t   *     circuitOpen            boolean — whether the alerts circuit breaker is open.\n915\t   *     unmatchedCount         number  — alerts that did not match any known trip.\n916\t   *\n917\t   *   delayDetector       object — delay detector subsystem status.\n918\t   *     trackedTrips           number — count of trips currently being tracked.\n919\t   *     activeAlerts           number — count of active predicted delay alerts.\n920\t   *     thresholdMultiplier    number — delay threshold configuration multiplier.\n921\t   *     minTrainsForLineAlert  number — minimum trains needed to trigger a line-level alert.\n922\t   *\n923\t   *   delayPredictor     object — delay predictor subsystem status.\n924\t   *     totalRecords           number — count of delay records stored.\n925\t   *     aggregatedPatterns     number — count of aggregated statistical patterns.\n926\t   *     minObservations        number — minimum observations required for a prediction.\n927\t   *     currentWeather         WeatherCondition — current weather string (e.g. \"clear\").\n928\t   *\n929\t   *   equipment           object (EquipmentCacheStatus) — elevator/escalator outage status.\n930\t   *     lastFetchAt            string | null — ISO 8601 of last fetch attempt.\n931\t   *     lastSuccessAt          string | null — ISO 8601 of last successful fetch.\n932\t   *     outageCount            number — number of stations with tracked outages.\n933\t   *     consecutiveFailures    number — consecutive equipment fetch failures.\n934\t   *     circuitOpen            boolean — whether the equipment circuit breaker is open.\n935\t   *\n936\t   *   pushSubscriptions   number — count of active push notification subscriptions.\n937\t   *\n938\t   *   cacheHitRate        number — fraction 0–1, computed as cache_hits_total /\n939\t   *                            (cache_hits_total + cache_misses_total) summed across all\n940\t   *                            label combinations. Rounded to 2 decimal places.\n941\t   *                            Returns 0 when there have been no cache requests.\n942\t   *\n943\t   *   memory             object — Node.js process memory snapshot via process.memoryUsage().\n944\t   *     rssBytes               number — resident set size in bytes.\n945\t   *     heapUsedBytes          number — active heap usage in bytes.\n946\t   *     heapTotalBytes         number — total heap allocated in bytes.\n947\t   *     externalBytes          number — external (C++/Buffer) memory in bytes.\n948\t   *\n949\t   *   failingFeedsCount  number — count of feeds where consecutiveFailures > 0 AND\n950\t   *                            lastSuccessAt !== null AND time since last success > 5 minutes.\n951\t   *\n952\t   * HTTP status code:\n953\t   *   200 — when fewer than UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD (3) feeds are failing\n954\t   *         (consecutiveFailures > 0, had prior success, last success > 5 min ago).\n955\t   *   503 — when 3 or more feeds meet the failing criteria above.\n956\t   *   Note: \"degraded\" status can coexist with a 200 response (e.g. one stale feed\n957\t   *         but fewer than 3 failing). 503 implies \"degraded\" but not vice versa.\n958\t   */\n959\t  app.get(\"/api/health\", (c) => {\n960\t    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n961\t    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n962\t    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n963\t    const feedStates = getFeedStates();\n964\t    const alertsStatus = getAlertsStatus();\n965\t    const allFeedsOk = !== null;\n969\t\n970\t    // Count feeds that have been failing for >5 minutes\n971\t    const now = Date.now();\n972\t    const failingFeeds = feedStates.filter(\n973\t      (f) =>\n974\t        f.consecutiveFailures > 0 && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && now - f.lastSuccessAt > 300_000\n975\t    );\n976\t    const unhealthy = failingFeeds.length >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD;\n977\t\n978\t    const status = allFeedsOk && alertsOk ? \"ok\" : \"degraded\";\n979\t    const httpStatus = unhealthy ? 503 : 200;\n980\t\n981\t    const memUsage = process.memoryUsage();\n982\t    const allMetrics = cacheHitsMap.values()) {\n991\t        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n992\t          cacheHitsValue += labeled.metric.value;\n993\t        }\n994\t      }\n995\t    }\n996\t    if (cacheMissesMap) {\n997\t      for (const labeled of cacheMissesMap.values()) {\n998\t        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n999\t          cacheMissesValue += labeled.metric.value;\n1000\t        }\n1001\t      }\n1002\t    }\n1003\t    const totalCacheRequests = cacheHitsValue + cacheMissesValue;\n1004\t    const cacheHitRate =\n1005\t      totalCacheRequests > 0 ? STATIC_CACHE_HEADER = `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTLS.gtfsStatic}, stale-while-revalidate=${CACHE_TTLS.gtfsStaticStale}`;\n167\t\n168\t/**\n169\t * Common abbreviation mappings for station search\n170\t */\n171\tconst ABBREVIATIONS: Record<string, string> = {\n172\t  sq: \"square\",\n173\t  st: \"street\",\n174\t  ave: \"avenue\",\n175\t  av: \"avenue\",\n176\t  blvd: \"boulevard\",\n177\t  pkwy: \"parkway\",\n178\t  rd: \"road\",\n179\t  dr: \"drive\",\n180\t  ln: \"lane\",\n181\t  ct: \"court\",\n182\t  pl: \"place\",\n183\t  hwy: \"highway\",\n184\t  expwy: \"expressway\",\n185\t  bway: \"broadway\",\n186\t  \"'way\": \"way\",\n187\t};\n188\t\n189\tfunction expandAbbreviations(term: string): string {\n190\t  const lower = term.toLowerCase();\n191\t  if (ABBREVIATIONS[lower]) {\n192\t    return ABBREVIATIONS[lower];\n193\t  }\n194\t  let expanded = term;\n195\t  for (const [abbr, full] of Object.entries(ABBREVIATIONS)) {\n196\t    const regex = new RegExp(`\\\\b${abbr}\\\\b`, \"gi\");\n197\t    expanded = expanded.replace(regex, full);\n198\t  }\n199\t  return expanded;\n200\t}\n201\t\n202\tfunction normalizeForSearch(str: string): string {\n203\t  return str\n204\t    .toLowerCase()\n205\t    .replace(/[^a-z0-9\\s]/g, \"\")\n206\t    .replace(/\\s+/g, \" \")\n207\t    .trim();\n208\t}\n209\t\n210\tfunction stationMatchesQuery(\n211\t  station: Station,\n212\t  normalizedQuery: string,\n213\t  originalQuery: string\n214\t): boolean {\n215\t  const normalizedName = normalizeForSearch(station.name);\n216\t  const expandedQuery = normalizeForSearch(expandAbbreviations(originalQuery));\n217\t  const expandedNormalizedQuery = normalizeForSearch(expandAbbreviations(normalizedQuery));\n218\t\n219\t  if (\n220\t    normalizedName.includes(normalizedQuery) ||\n221\t    normalizedName.includes(expandedQuery) ||\n222\t    normalizedName.includes(expandedNormalizedQuery)\n223\t  ) {\n224\t    return true;\n225\t  }\n226\t\n227\t  const upperQuery = originalQuery.toUpperCase();\n228\t  if (station.lines.some((line) => line === upperQuery)) {\n229\t    return true;\n230\t  }\n231\t\n232\t  const expandedName = normalizeForSearch(expandAbbreviations(station.name));\n233\t  if (expandedName.includes(expandedQuery) || expandedName.includes(expandedNormalizedQuery)) {\n234\t    return true;\n235\t  }\n236\t\n237\t  return false;\n238\t}\n239\t\n240\tfunction scoreSearchResult(station: Station, query: string): number {\n241\t  const normalizedQuery = normalizeForSearch(query);\n242\t  const normalizedName = normalizeForSearch(station.name);\n243\t  const upperQuery = query.toUpperCase();\n244\t\n245\t  if (station.lines.some((line) => line === upperQuery)) {\n246\t    return 1000;\n247\t  }\n248\t\n249\t  if (normalizedName.startsWith(normalizedQuery)) {\n250\t    return 100;\n251\t  }\n252\t\n253\t  const words = normalizedName.split(/\\s+/);\n254\t  if (words.some((word) => word.startsWith(normalizedQuery))) {\n255\t    return 50;\n256\t  }\n257\t\n258\t  if (normalizedName.includes(normalizedQuery)) {\n259\t    return 10;\n260\t  }\n261\t\n262\t  return 1;\n263\t}\n264\t\n265\tfunction buildStationToComplexMap(complexes: ComplexIndex): Map<string, StationComplex> {\n266\t  const map = new Map<string, StationComplex>();\n267\t  for (const complex of Object.values(complexes)) {\n268\t    for (const stationId of complex.stations) {\n269\t      map.set(stationId, complex);\n270\t    }\n271\t  }\n272\t  return map;\n273\t}\n274\t\n275\t/**\n276\t * @param stations         Pre-loaded GTFS static station index\n277\t * @param routes           Pre-loaded GTFS static route index\n278\t * @param complexes        Pre-loaded station complexes index\n279\t * @param transfers        Pre-loaded transfer connections\n280\t * @param webDistPath      Absolute path to the built React PWA (packages/web/dist)\n281\t */\n282\t/** Cache header for immutable assets (hashed filenames) */\n283\tconst IMMUTABLE_CACHE_HEADER = \"public, max-age=31536000, immutable\";\n284\t\n285\t/**\n286\t * Compression middleware for API responses.\n287\t * Supports brotli, gzip, and deflate based on Accept-Encoding header.\n288\t * Brotli is preferred for best compression ratio.\n289\t */\n290\tfunction compressionMiddleware(): MiddlewareHandler {\n291\t  return async (c, next) => {\n292\t    await next();\n293\t\n294\t    const body = c.res.body;\n295\t    if (!body || c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Encoding\")) return;\n296\t\n297\t    const contentType = c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Type\");\n298\t    if (!contentType?.includes(\"json\") && !contentType?.includes(\"text\")) return;\n299\t\n300\t    const acceptEncoding = c.req.header(\"Accept-Encoding\") || \"\";\n301\t    const contentLength = c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Length\");\n302\t\n303\t    // Skip small responses (not worth compressing)\n304\t    if (contentLength && parseInt(contentLength, 10) < 500) return;\n305\t\n306\t    // Clone response to get the body as buffer\n307\t    const buffer = Buffer.from(await c.res.clone().arrayBuffer());\n308\t\n309\t    // Prefer brotli for best compression, then gzip, then deflate\n310\t    if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"br\")) {\n311\t      const brotlied = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n312\t        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n313\t        const brotli = createBrotliCompress();\n314\t        brotli.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n315\t        brotli.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n316\t        brotli.on(\"error\", reject);\n317\t        brotli.end(buffer);\n318\t      });\n319\t\n320\t      c.res = new Response(brotlied, {\n321\t        status: c.res.status,\n322\t        headers: c.res.headers,\n323\t      });\n324\t      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"br\");\n325\t      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n326\t    } else if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"gzip\")) {\n327\t      const gzipped = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n328\t        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n329\t        const gzip = createGzip();\n330\t        gzip.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n331\t        gzip.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n332\t        gzip.on(\"error\", reject);\n333\t        gzip.end(buffer);\n334\t      });\n335\t\n336\t      c.res = new Response(gzipped, {\n337\t        status: c.res.status,\n338\t        headers: c.res.headers,\n339\t      });\n340\t      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"gzip\");\n341\t      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n342\t    } else if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"deflate\")) {\n343\t      const deflated = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n344\t        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n345\t        const deflate = createDeflate();\n346\t        deflate.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n347\t        deflate.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n348\t        deflate.on(\"error\", reject);\n349\t        deflate.end(buffer);\n350\t      });\n351\t\n352\t      c.res = new Response(deflated, {\n353\t        status: c.res.status,\n354\t        headers: c.res.headers,\n355\t      });\n356\t      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"deflate\");\n357\t      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n358\t    }\n359\t  };\n360\t}\n361\t\n362\t/**\n363\t * Check if a path has a content hash (for immutable caching).\n364\t * Vite produces files like: assets/index-a1b2c3d4.js\n365\t */\n366\tfunction isHashedAsset(path: string): boolean {\n367\t  // Match pattern: /assets/name-[hash].ext where hash is 8+ hex chars\n368\t  return /\\/assets\\/[^/]+-[a-f0-9]{8,}\\.(js|css|svg|png|jpg|ico|woff2?)$/i.test(path);\n369\t}\n370\t\n371\texport function createApp(\n372\t  stations: StationIndex,\n373\t  routes: RouteIndex,\n374\t  complexes: ComplexIndex,\n375\t  transfers: Record<string, TransferConnection[]>,\n376\t  webDistPath: string\n377\t): app.use(\"*\", requestId);\n383\t\n384\t  // Security headers on all responses (CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy)\n385\t  // CSP includes report-uri for violation function timeAgo(isoString: string | null): string {\n526\t    if (!isoString) return \"Never\";\n527\t    const ms = Date.now() - new Date(isoString).getTime();\n528\t    const seconds = Math.floor(ms / 1000);\n529\t    if (seconds < 60) return `${seconds}s ago`;\n530\t    const minutes = Math.floor(seconds / 60);\n531\t    if (minutes < 60) return `${minutes}m ago`;\n532\t    const  * Format bytes as human-readable (e.g., \"45.2 MB\")\n540\t   */\n541\t  function formatBytes(bytes: number): string {\n542\t    const units = [\"B\", \"KB\", \"MB\", \"GB\"];\n543\t    let i = 0;\n544\t    while (bytes >= 1024 && i < units.length - 1) {\n545\t      bytes /= 1024;\n546\t      i++;\n547\t    }\n548\t    return `${bytes.toFixed(1)} ${units[i]}`;\n549\t  }\n550\t\n551\t Subscriptions</div>\n739\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">${healthData.pushSubscriptions}</div>\n740\t          </div>\n741\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n742\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">Failing Feeds</div>\n743\t            <div class=\"metric-value\" style=\"color: ${healthData.failingFeedsCount > 0 ? \"#ef4444\" : \"#22c55e\"}\">${healthData.failingFeedsCount}</div>\n744\t          </div>\n745\t        </div>\n746\t      </div>\n747\t\n748\t      <!-- Memory Usage -->\n749\t      <div class=\"card\">\n750\t        <div class=\"card-title\">Memory Usage</div>\n751\t        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n752\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n753\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">RSS (Total)</div>\n754\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">${formatBytes(healthData.memory.rssBytes)}</div>\n755\t          </div>\n756\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n757\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">Heap ${formatBytes(healthData.memory.heapTotalBytes)}</div>\n760\t          </div>\n761\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n762\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">External</div>\n763\t            <div (24h)</th>\n780\t            </tr>\n781\t          </thead>\n782\t          <tbody>\n783\t            ${healthData.feeds\n784\t              .map(\n785\t                (feed) => `\n786\t            <tr>\n787\t              <td>${feed.name}</td>\n788\t              <td><span class=\"status-badge status-${feed.status}\">${feed.status.replace(/_/g, \" \")}</span></td>\n789\t              <td>${timeAgo(feed.lastSuccessAt)}</td>\n790\t              <td>${feed.entityCount}</td>\n791\t              <td>${feed.avgLatencyMs}ms</td>\n792\t              <td>${feed.errorCount24h}</td>\n793\t            </tr>\n794\t          `\n795\t              )\n796\t              .join(\"\")}\n797\t          </tbody>\n798\t        </table>\n799\t      </div>\n800\t\n801\t      <!-- Alerts Status <div class=\"metric-label\">Active Alerts</div>\n807\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">${healthData.alerts.count}</div>\n808\t          </div>\n809\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n810\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">Match Rate</div>\n811\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">${(healthData.alerts.matchRate * 100).toFixed(0)}%</div>\n812\t            <div class=\"metric-sub\">${healthData.alerts.unmatchedCount} unmatched</div>\n813\t          </div>\n814\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n815\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">Last Update</div>\n816\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">${timeAgo(healthData.alerts.lastSuccessAt)}</div>\n817\t          </div>\n818\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n819\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">Status</div>\n820\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">\n821\t              <span class=\"status-badge status-${healthData.alerts.circuitOpen ? \"circuit_open\" : \"ok\"}\">\n822\t                ${healthData.alerts.circuitOpen ? \"Circuit Open\" : \"OK\"}\n823\t              </span>\n824\t            </div>\n825\t          </div>\n826\t        </div>\n827\t      </div>\n828\t\n829\t      <!-- Delay Detection -->\n830\t      <div class=\"card\">\n831\t        <div class=\"card-title\">Delay Detection</div>\n832\t        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n833\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n834\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">Detector</div>\n835\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">\n836\t              <span class=\"status-badge       </div>\n840\t            <div class=\"metric-sub\">${healthData.delayDetector.activePredictions} active predictions</div>\n841\t          </div>\n842\t          <div class=\"metric\">\n843\t            <div class=\"metric-label\">Predictor</div>\n844\t            <div class=\"metric-value\">\n845\t              <span class=\"status-badge status-${healthData.delayPredictor.enabled ? \"ok\" :    <a href=\"/api/health\" style=\"color: #38bdf8;\">View JSON</a>\n863\t    </div>\n864\t  </div>\n865\t</body>\n866\t</html>`;\n867\t  }\n868\t\n869\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n870\t  // Health endpoint\n871\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n872\t  /**\n873\t   * GET /api/health — Per-feed health status (JSON)\n874\t   *\n875\t   * Query validation: Uses `emptyQuerySchema` (`z.object({}).strict()`).\n876\t   * Any query parameters at all cause a 400 with `{ error: \"validation failed\", details: [...] }`.\n877\t   * No query parameters are accepted.\n878\t   *\n879\t   * Response top-level fields:\n880\t   *   status              (\"ok\" | \"degraded\")\n881\t   *     — \"ok\" when all feeds have circuitOpenAt===null AND lastSuccessAt!==null AND !isStale,\n882\t   *       AND alerts has circuitOpen===false AND lastSuccessAt!==null.\n883\t   *     — \"degraded\" otherwise.\n884\t   *\n885\t   *   timestamp           string (ISO 8601) — new Date().toISOString() at request time.\n886\t   *\n887\t   *   uptime_seconds      number — seconds since SERVER_START_MS (set at module load).\n888\t   *\n889\t   *   feeds               Array<FeedState> — one entry per GTFS-realtime feed.\n890\t   *     Each element:\n891\t   *       id                    string   — feed identifier (e.g. \"1\", \"26\", \"31\", \"36\").\n892\t   *       name                  string   — human-readable feed name.\n893\t   *       status                (\"circuit_open\" | \"never_polled\" | \"stale\" | \"ok\")\n894\t   *         — \"circuit_open\" when circuitOpenAt !== null.\n895\t   *         — \"never_polled\" when lastSuccessAt === null.\n896\t   *         — \"stale\" when isStale === true.\n897\t   *         — \"ok\" otherwise.\n898\t   *       lastSuccessAt         string | null — ISO 8601 of last successful poll, or null.\n899\t   *       lastPollAt            string | null — ISO 8601 of last poll attempt, or null.\n900\t   *       consecutiveFailures  number — count of consecutive failed polls.\n901\t   *       entityCount           number — number of entities in the feed.\n902\t   *       lastError             string | null — truncated (max 100 chars) last error message,\n903\t   *                                  newlines stripped; null if no error.\n904\t   *       tripReplacementPeriod string | null — the feed's trip replacement period.\n905\t   *       avgLatencyMs          number — average of latencyHistory entries.\n906\t   *       errorCount24h         number — count of error timestamps within the last 24 hours.\n907\t   *       parseErrors           (feed-specific type) — parse error details for the feed.\n908\t   *\n909\t   *   alerts              object — alerts polling subsystem status.\n910\t   *     count                  number  — number of alerts tracked.\n911\t   *     lastSuccessAt          number | null — epoch-ms of last successful alert fetch, or null.\n912\t   *     matchRate              number  — fraction of alerts that matched trips (0–1).\n913\t   *     consecutiveFailures    number  — consecutive alert fetch failures.\n914\t   *     circuitOpen            boolean — whether the alerts circuit breaker is open.\n915\t   *     unmatchedCount         number  — alerts that did not match any known trip.\n916\t   *\n917\t   *   delayDetector       object — delay detector subsystem status.\n918\t   *     trackedTrips           number — count of trips currently being tracked.\n919\t   *     activeAlerts           number — count of active predicted delay alerts.\n920\t   *     thresholdMultiplier    number — delay threshold configuration multiplier.\n921\t   *     minTrainsForLineAlert  number — minimum trains needed to trigger a line-level alert.\n922\t   *\n923\t   *   delayPredictor     object — delay predictor subsystem status.\n924\t   *     totalRecords           number — count of delay records stored.\n925\t   *     aggregatedPatterns     number — count of aggregated statistical patterns.\n926\t   *     minObservations        number — minimum observations required for a prediction.\n927\t   *     currentWeather         WeatherCondition — current weather string (e.g. \"clear\").\n928\t   *\n929\t   *   equipment           object (EquipmentCacheStatus) — elevator/escalator outage status.\n930\t   *     lastFetchAt            string | null — ISO 8601 of last fetch attempt.\n931\t   *     lastSuccessAt          string | null — ISO 8601 of last successful fetch.\n932\t   *     outageCount            number — number of stations with tracked outages.\n933\t   *     consecutiveFailures    number — consecutive equipment fetch failures.\n934\t   *     circuitOpen            boolean — whether the equipment circuit breaker is open.\n935\t   *\n936\t   *   pushSubscriptions   number — count of active push notification subscriptions.\n937\t   *\n938\t   *   cacheHitRate        number — fraction 0–1, computed as cache_hits_total /\n939\t   *                            (cache_hits_total + cache_misses_total) summed across all\n940\t   *                            label combinations. Rounded to 2 decimal places.\n941\t   *                            Returns 0 when there have been no cache requests.\n942\t   *\n943\t   *   memory             object — Node.js process memory snapshot via process.memoryUsage().\n944\t   *     rssBytes               number — resident set size in bytes.\n945\t   *     heapUsedBytes          number — active heap usage in bytes.\n946\t   *     heapTotalBytes         number — total heap allocated in bytes.\n947\t   *     externalBytes          number — external (C++/Buffer) memory in bytes.\n948\t   *\n949\t   *   failingFeedsCount  number — count of feeds where consecutiveFailures > 0 AND\n950\t   *                            lastSuccessAt !== null AND time since last success > 5 minutes.\n951\t   *\n952\t   * HTTP status code:\n953\t   *   200 — when fewer than UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD (3) feeds are failing\n954\t   *         (consecutiveFailures > 0, had prior success, last success > 5 min ago).\n955\t   *   503 — when 3 or more feeds meet the failing criteria above.\n956\t   *   Note: \"degraded\" status can coexist with a 200 response (e.g. one stale feed\n957\t   *         but fewer than 3 failing). 503 implies \"degraded\" but not vice versa.\n958\t   */\n959\t  app.get(\"/api/health\", (c) => {\n960\t    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n961\t    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n962\t    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n963\t    const feedStates = getFeedStates();\n964\t    const alertsStatus = getAlertsStatus();\n965\t    const allFeedsOk = feedStates.every(\n966\t      (f) => f.circuitOpenAt === null && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && !f.isStale\n967\t    );\n968\t    const alertsOk = !alertsStatus.circuitOpen && alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt !== null;\n969\t\n970\t    // Count feeds that have been failing for >5 minutes\n971\t    const now = Date.now();\n972\t    const failingFeeds = feedStates.filter(\n973\t      (f) =>\n974\t        f.consecutiveFailures > 0 && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && now - f.lastSuccessAt > 300_000\n975\t    );\n976\t    const unhealthy = failingFeeds.length >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD;\n977\t\n978\t    const status = allFeedsOk && alertsOk ? \"ok\" : \"degraded\";\n979\t    const httpStatus = unhealthy ? 503 : 200;\n980\t\n981\t    const memUsage = process.memoryUsage();\n982\t    const allMetrics = metrics.getAll();\n983\t\n984\t    // Get cache hit rate from metrics (sum across all label combinations)\n985\t    let cacheHitsValue = 0;\n986\t    let cacheMissesValue = 0;\n987\t    const cacheHitsMap.values()) {\n991\t        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n992\t          cacheHitsValue += labeled.metric.value;\n993\t        }\n994\t      }\n995\t    }\n996\t    if (cacheMissesMap) {\n997\t      for (const labeled of cacheMissesMap.values()) {\n998\t        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n999\t          cacheMissesValue += labeled.metric.value;\n1000\t        }\n1001\t      }\n1002\t    }\n1003\t    const totalCacheRequests = cacheHitsValue + cacheMissesValue;\n1004\t    const cacheHitRate =\n1005\t      totalCacheRequests > 0 ? Math.round((cacheHitsValue / totalCacheRequests) * 100) / 100 : 0;\n1006\t\n1007\t    return c.json(\n1008\t      {\n1009\t        status,\n1010\t        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n1011\t        uptime_seconds: Math.floor((Date.now() - SERVER_START_MS) / 1000),\n1012\t        feeds: feedStates.map((f) => ({\n1013\t          id: f.id,\n1014\t          name: f.name,\n1015\t          status:\n1016\t            f.circuitOpenAt !== null\n1017\t              ? \"circuit_open\"\n1018\t              : f.lastSuccessAt === null\n1019\t                ? \"never_polled\"\n1020\t                : f.isStale\n1021\t                  ? \"stale\"\n1022\t                  : \"ok\",\n1023\t          lastSuccessAt: f.lastSuccessAt ? new Date(f.lastSuccessAt).toISOString() : null,\n1024\t          lastPollAt: f.lastPollAt ? new Date(f.lastPollAt).toISOString() : null,\n1025\t          consecutiveFailures: f.consecutiveFailures,\n1026\t          entityCount: f.entityCount,\n1027\t          lastError: f.lastErrorMessage\n1028\t            ? f.lastErrorMessage.slice(0, 100).replace(/[\\r\\n]/g, \" \")\n1029\t            : null,\n1030\t          tripReplacementPeriod: f.tripReplacementPeriod,\n1031\t          avgLatencyMs: avgLatency(f.latencyHistory),\n1032\t          errorCount24h: errorCount24h(f.errorTimestamps),\n1033\t          parseErrors: f.parseErrors,\n1034\t        })),\n1035\t        alerts: {\n1036\t          count: alertsStatus.alertCount,\n1037\t          lastSuccessAt: alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt,\n1038\t          matchRate: alertsStatus.matchRate,\n1039\t          consecutiveFailures: alertsStatus.consecutiveFailures,\n1040\t          circuitOpen: alertsStatus.circuitOpen,\n1041\t          unmatchedCount: alertsStatus.unmatchedCount,\n1042\t        },\n1043\t        delayDetector: getDelayDetectorStatus(),\n1044\t        delayPredictor: getDelayPredictorStatus(),\n1045\t        equipment: getEquipmentStatus(),\n1046\t        pushSubscriptions: getSubscriptionCount(),\n1047\t        cacheHitRate,\n1048\t        memory: {\n1049\t          rssBytes: memUsage.rss,\n1050\t          heapUsedBytes: memUsage.heapUsed,\n1051\t          heapTotalBytes: memUsage.heapTotal,\n1052\t          externalBytes: memUsage.external,\n1053\t        },\n1054\t        failingFeedsCount: failingFeeds.length,\n1055\t      },\n1056\t      httpStatus as 200 | 503\n1057\t    );\n1058\t  });\n1059\t\n1060\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1180\t  // CSP violation reporting endpoint\n1181\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1182\t  app.post(\"/api/security/csp-report\", async (c) => {\n1183\t    try {\n1184\t      const contentType = c.req.header(\"Content-Type\") ?? \"\";\n1185\t      const baseContentType = contentType.split(\";\")[0]!.trim().toLowerCase();\n1186\t      const allowed = [\"application/csp-report\", \"application/json\", \"application/reports+json\"];\n1187\t      if (!allowed.includes(baseContentType)) {\n1188\t        return c.json({ error: \"Unsupported Media Type\" }, 415);\n1189\t      }\n1190\t\n1191\t      const report = await c.req.json().catch(() => null);\n1192\t\n1193\t      if (!report) {\n1194\t        return c.json({ error: \"Invalid report\" }, 400);\n1195\t      }\n1196\t\n1197\t      // Log the CSP violation for security monitoring\n1198\t      logger.warn(\"CSP violation detected\", {\n1199\t        \"user-agent\": c.req.header(\"user-agent\"),\n1200\t        referrer: c.req.header(\"referrer\"),\n1201\t        \"x-forwarded-for\": c.req.header(\"x-forwarded-for\")?.split(\",\")[0] ?? \"unknown\",\n1202\t        report,\n1203\t      });\n1204\t\n1205\t      // Return 200 to acknowledge the report\n1206\t      return c.json({ received: true });\n1207\t    } catch (error) {\n1208\t      logger.error(\"Failed to process CSP report\", error as Error);\n1209\t      return c.json({ error: \"Failed to process report\" }, 400);\n1210\t    }\n1211\t  });\n1212\t\n1213\t  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1214\t  // Security disclosure policy (RFC 9116)\n1215\t  // stale-while-revalidate=${CACHE_TTLS.gtfsStaticStale}`;\n\n/**\n * Common abbreviation mappings for station search\n */\nconst ABBREVIATIONS: Record<string, string> = {\n  sq: \"square\",\n  st: \"street\",\n  ave: \"avenue\",\n  av: \"avenue\",\n  blvd: \"boulevard\",\n  pkwy: \"parkway\",\n  rd: \"road\",\n  dr: \"drive\",\n  ln: \"lane\",\n  ct: \"court\",\n  pl: \"place\",\n  hwy: \"highway\",\n  expwy: \"expressway\",\n  bway: \"broadway\",\n  \"'way\": \"way\",\n};\n\nfunction expandAbbreviations(term: string): string {\n  const lower = term.toLowerCase();\n  if (ABBREVIATIONS[lower]) {\n    return ABBREVIATIONS[lower];\n  }\n  let expanded = term;\n  for (const [abbr, full] of Object.entries(ABBREVIATIONS)) {\n    const regex = new RegExp(`\\\\b${abbr}\\\\b`, \"gi\");\n    expanded = expanded.replace(regex, full);\n  }\n  return expanded;\n}\n\nfunction normalizeForSearch(str: string): string {\n  return str\n    .toLowerCase()\n    .replace(/[^a-z0-9\\s]/g, \"\")\n    .replace(/\\s+/g, \" \")\n    .trim();\n}\n\nfunction stationMatchesQuery(\n  station: Station,\n  normalizedQuery: string,\n  originalQuery: string\n): boolean {\n  const normalizedName = normalizeForSearch(station.name);\n  const expandedQuery = normalizeForSearch(expandAbbreviations(originalQuery));\n  const expandedNormalizedQuery = normalizeForSearch(expandAbbreviations(normalizedQuery));\n\n  if (\n    normalizedName.includes(normalizedQuery) ||\n    normalizedName.includes(expandedQuery) ||\n    normalizedName.includes(expandedNormalizedQuery)\n  ) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  const upperQuery = originalQuery.toUpperCase();\n  if (station.lines.some((line) => line === upperQuery)) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  const expandedName = normalizeForSearch(expandAbbreviations(station.name));\n  if (expandedName.includes(expandedQuery) || expandedName.includes(expandedNormalizedQuery)) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  return false;\n}\n\nfunction scoreSearchResult(station: Station, query: string): number {\n  const normalizedQuery = normalizeForSearch(query);\n  const normalizedName = normalizeForSearch(station.name);\n  const upperQuery = query.toUpperCase();\n\n  if (station.lines.some((line) => line === upperQuery)) {\n    return 1000;\n  }\n\n  if (normalizedName.startsWith(normalizedQuery)) {\n    return 100;\n  }\n\n  const words = normalizedName.split(/\\s+/);\n  if (words.some((word) => word.startsWith(normalizedQuery))) {\n    return 50;\n  }\n\n  if (normalizedName.includes(normalizedQuery)) {\n    return 10;\n  }\n\n  return 1;\n}\n\nfunction buildStationToComplexMap(complexes: ComplexIndex): Map<string, StationComplex> {\n  const map = new Map<string, StationComplex>();\n  for (const complex of Object.values(complexes)) {\n    for (const stationId of complex.stations) {\n      map.set(stationId, complex);\n    }\n  }\n  return map;\n}\n\n/**\n * @param stations         Pre-loaded GTFS static station index\n * @param routes           Pre-loaded GTFS static route index\n * @param complexes        Pre-loaded station complexes (hashed filenames) */\nconst IMMUTABLE_CACHE_HEADER = \"public, max-age=31536000, immutable\";\n\n/**\n * Compression middleware for API responses.\n * Supports brotli, gzip, and deflate based on Accept-Encoding header.\n * Brotli is preferred for best compression ratio.\n */\nfunction compressionMiddleware(): MiddlewareHandler {\n  return async (c, next) => {\n    await next();\n\n    const body = c.res.body;\n    if (!body || c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Encoding\")) return;\n\n    const contentType = c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Type\");\n    if (!contentType?.includes(\"json\") && !contentType?.includes(\"text\")) return;\n\n    const acceptEncoding = c.req.header(\"Accept-Encoding\") || \"\";\n    const contentLength = c.res.headers.get(\"Content-Length\");\n\n    // Skip small responses (not worth compressing)\n    if (contentLength && parseInt(contentLength, 10) < 500) return;\n\n    // Clone response to get the body as buffer\n    const buffer = Buffer.from(await c.res.clone().arrayBuffer());\n\n    // Prefer brotli for best compression, then gzip, then deflate\n    if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"br\")) {\n      const brotlied = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n        const brotli = createBrotliCompress();\n        brotli.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n        brotli.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n        brotli.on(\"error\", reject);\n        brotli.end(buffer);\n      });\n\n      c.res = new Response(brotlied, {\n        status: c.res.status,\n        headers: c.res.headers,\n      });\n      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"br\");\n      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n    } else if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"gzip\")) {\n      const gzipped = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n        const gzip = createGzip();\n        gzip.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n        gzip.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n        gzip.on(\"error\", reject);\n        gzip.end(buffer);\n      });\n\n      c.res = new Response(gzipped, {\n        status: c.res.status,\n        headers: c.res.headers,\n      });\n      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"gzip\");\n      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n    } else if (acceptEncoding.includes(\"deflate\")) {\n      const deflated = await new Promise<Buffer>((resolve, reject) => {\n        const chunks: Buffer[] = [];\n        const deflate = createDeflate();\n        deflate.on(\"data\", (chunk) => chunks.push(chunk));\n        deflate.on(\"end\", () => resolve(Buffer.concat(chunks)));\n        deflate.on(\"error\", reject);\n        deflate.end(buffer);\n      });\n\n      c.res = new Response(deflated, {\n        status: c.res.status,\n        headers: c.res.headers,\n      });\n      c.res.headers.set(\"Content-Encoding\", \"deflate\");\n      c.res.headers.delete(\"Content-Length\");\n    }\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a path has a content hash (for immutable caching).\n * Vite produces files like: assets/index-a1b2c3d4.js\n */\nfunction isHashedAsset(path: string): boolean {\n  // Match pattern: /assets/name-[hash].ext where hash is 8+ hex chars\n  return /\\/assets\\/[^/]+-[a-f0-9]{8,}\\.(js|css|svg|png|jpg|ico|woff2?)$/i.test(path);\n}\n\nexport function createApp(\n  stations: StationIndex,\n  routes: RouteIndex,\n  complexes: ComplexIndex,\n  transfers: Record<string, TransferConnection[]>,\n  webDistPath: string\n): Hono {\n  const app = new Hono();\n\n  // Request ID for correlation across logs and audit events.\n  // Must run before security logging and audit middleware.\n  app.use(\"*\", requestId);\n\n  // Security headers on all responses (CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy)\n  // CSP includes for all requests (OWASP A09: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures)\n  // Logs authentication failures, authorization failures, rate limit exceeded, and blocked attacks\n  app.use(\"*\", securityLogging());\n\n  // HTTP method restrictions (OWASP A01, A05)\n  // Blocks dangerous HTTP methods (TRACE, CONNECT) that could be used for attacks\n  app.use(\"*\", httpMethodRestrictions());\n\n  // HTTP Request Smuggling protection (OWASP A01, A03)\n  // Detects and blocks request smuggling attempts\n  app.use(\"*\", httpRequestSmuggling());\n\n  // HTTP Response Splitting protection (OWASP A01, A03)\n  // Detects and blocks CRLF injection attempts\n  app.use(\"*\", httpResponseSplitting());\n\n  // Host header protection to prevent cache poisoning and password reset poisoning\n  // In production, set ALLOWED_HOSTS environment variable to restrict allowed hosts\n  const isProduction = process.env[\"NODE_ENV\"] === \"production\";\n  const allowedHosts = process.env[\"ALLOWED_HOSTS\"]\n    ? process.env[\"ALLOWED_HOSTS\"].split(\",\")\n    : undefined;\n  app.use(\n    \"*\",\n    hostHeaderProtection({\n      allowedHosts,\n app.use(\"*\", requestSizeLimits());\n\n  // Input sanitization for all API routes (XSS, SQL injection prevention)\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", inputSanitization());\n\n  // JSON depth protection for API routes (OWASP A01, A03)\n  // Prevents DoS via deeply nested JSON structures\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", jsonDepthProtection());\n\n  // Optional authentication for all API routes\n  // Parses Authorization header and sets auth context if present\n  // Individual routes can require specific permissions using requirePermission\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", optionalAuth({ allowSessions: true }));\n\n  // CSRF protection for state-changing operations\n  // Excludes health, metrics, and safe read-only endpoints\n  // NOTE: /status is a public read-only HTML page (not under /api/*, so not affected)\n  // NOTE: /api/context, /api/auth/oauth, /api/auth/mfa, /api/auth/session are disabled\n  // NOTE: /api/auth/password is enabled for password reset functionality\n  app.use(\n    \"/api/*\",\n    csrfProtection({\n      excludePaths: [\n        \"/api/health\",\n        \"/api/metrics\",\n        \"/api/stations\",\n        \"/api/routes\",\n        \"/api/static\",\n        \"/api/arrivals\",\n        \"/api/alerts\",\n        \"/api/equipment\",\n        \"/api/trip\",\n        \"/api/positions\",\n        \"/api/push/vapid-public-key\",\n        \"/api/journal\",\n        // \"/api/context\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n        // \"/api/auth/oauth\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n        // \"/api/auth/mfa\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n        // \"/api/auth/session\", // DISABLED: Feature not used by frontend\n        \"/api/auth/password\",\n        \"/api/csrf-token\",\n        \"/api/security/csp-report\", // CSP violation reports (browser-initiated)\n      ],\n    })\n  );\n\n  // HPP protection for all API routes (prevents parameter pollution attacks)\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", hppProtection({ strategy: \"first\" }));\n\n  // HTTP metrics collection for all API routes\n  app.use(\"/api/*\", httpMetrics());\n\n  // Rate limiting on all API routes (60 req/min per IP, token bucket)\n });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // CSRF token endpoint\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\n    \"/api/csrf-token\",\n    cors({\n      allowedOrigins: [\"*\"], // Allow all origins for CSRF token\n      allowedMethods: [\"GET\"],\n      allowedHeaders: [\"Content-Type\", \"Authorization\"],\n    }),\n    (c) => {\n      const token = generateCsrfToken();\n\n      return c.json({ token });\n    }\n  );\n\n  /**\n   * Format a timestamp as \"X ago\" for human-readable display\n   */\n  function timeAgo(isoString: string | null): string {\n    if (!isoString) return \"Never\";\n    const ms = Date.now() - new Date(isoString).getTime();\n    const seconds = Math.floor(ms / 1000);\n    if (seconds < 60) return `${seconds}s ago`;\n    const minutes = Math.floor(seconds / 60);\n    if (minutes < 60) return `${minutes}m ago`;\n    const hours = Math.floor(minutes / 60);\n    if (hours < 24) return `${hours}h ago`;\n    const days = Math.floor(hours / 24);\n    return `${days}d ago`;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Format bytes as human-readable (e.g., \"45.2 MB\")\n   */\n  function formatBytes(bytes: number): string {\n    const units = [\"B\", \"KB\", \"MB\", \"GB\"];\n    let i = 0;\n    while (bytes >= 1024 && i < units.length - 1) {\n      bytes /= 1024;\n      i++;\n    }\n    return `${bytes.toFixed(1)} ${units[i]}`;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Build the HTML response for the /status dashboard\n   */\n  function buildStatusHtml(healthData:
