alerts: {\n      count: number;\n      lastSuccessAt: string | null;\n      matchRate: number;\n      consecutiveFailures: number;\n      circuitOpen: boolean;\n      unmatchedCount: number;\n    };\n    delayDetector: {\n      enabled: boolean;\n      activePredictions: number;\n      lastRunAt: string | null;\n    };\n    delayPredictor: {\n      enabled: boolean;\n      modelAccuracy: number;\n      lastTrainedAt: string | null;\n    };\n    equipment: {\n      lastSuccessAt: string | null;\n      outages: number;\n      consecutiveFailures: number;\n    };\n    pushSubscriptions: number;\n    cacheHitRate: number;\n    memory: {\n      rssBytes: number;\n      heapUsedBytes: number;\n      heapTotalBytes: number;\n      externalBytes: number;\n    };\n    failingFeedsCount: number;\n  }): string {\n    const uptimeHours = Math.floor(healthData.uptime_seconds / 3600);\n    const uptimeMinutes = Math.floor((healthData.uptime_seconds % 3600) / 60);\n    const statusColor =\n      healthData.status === \"ok\"\n        ? \"#22c55e\"\n        : healthData.failingFeedsCount >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD\n          ? \"#ef4444\"\n          : \"#f59e0b\";\n    const statusIcon =\n      healthData.status === \"ok\"\n        ? \"✓\"\n        : healthData.failingFeedsCount >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD\n          ? \"✗\"\n          : \"⚠\";\n\n    return `<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"en\">\n<head>\n  <meta charset=\"UTF-8\">\n  <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\">\n  <title>MTA My Way - System Status</title>\n  <style>\n    * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; }\n    body {\n      font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, \"Segoe UI\", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;\n      background: #0f172a;\n      color: #e2e8f0;\n      line-height: 1.5;\n      padding: 20px;\n    }\n    .container { max-width: 1200px; margin: 0 auto; }\n    h1 { font-size: 24px; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 8px; }\n    .subtitle { color: #94a3b8; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 24px; }\n    .status-banner {\n      display: inline-flex;\n      align-items: center;\n      gap: 8px;\n      padding: 12px 20px;\n      border-radius: 8px;\n      font-weight: 600;\n      font-size: 16px;\n      margin-bottom: 24px;\n      background: ${statusColor}20;\n      border: 1px solid ${statusColor}40;\n    }\n    .status-icon { font-size: 20px; }\n    .status-text { color: ${statusColor}; }\n    .grid { display: grid; gap: 20px; }\n    .card {\n      background: #1e293b;\n      border-radius: 12px;\n      padding: 20px;\n      border: 1px solid #334155;\n    }\n    .card-title {\n      font-size: 16px;\n      font-weight: 600;\n      margin-bottom: 16px;\n      color: #f1f5f9;\n    }\n    .metric-grid {\n      display: grid;\n      grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(200px, 1fr));\n      gap: 16px;\n    }\n    .metric { background: #0f172a; padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; }\n    .metric-label { font-size: 12px; color: #94a3b8; margin-bottom: 4px; }\n    .metric-value { font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600; color: #f1f5f9; }\n    .metric-sub { font-size: 12px; color: #64748b; margin-top: 4px; }\n    .feed-table {\n      width: 100%;\n      border-collapse: collapse;\n      font-size: 14px;\n    }\n    .feed-table th {\n      text-align: left;\n      padding: 12px;\n      border-bottom: 1px solid #334155;\n      color: #94a3b8;\n      font-weight: 500;\n    }\n    .feed-table td {\n      padding: 12px;\n      border-bottom: 1px solid #1e293b;\n    }\n    .feed-table tr:last-child td { border-bottom: none; }\n    .status-badge {\n      display: inline-block;\n      padding: 4px 10px;\n      border-radius: 12px;\n      font-size: 12px;\n      font-weight: 500;\n    }\n    .status-ok { background: #22c55e20; color: #22c55e; }\n    .status-stale { background: #f59e0b20; color: #f59e0b; }\n    .status-circuit_open { background: #ef444420; color: #ef4444; }\n    .status-never_polled { background: #64748b20; color: #94a3b8; }\n    .last-updated {\n      text-align: center;\n      color: #64748b;\n      font-size: 12px;\n      margin-top: 32px;\n      padding-top: 16px;\n      border-top: 1px solid #1e293b;\n    }\n    @media (max-width: 640px) {\n      .feed-table { display: block; overflow-x: auto; }\n      .metric-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }\n    }\n  </style>\n</head>\n<body>\n  <div class=\"container\">\n    <h1>MTA My Way</h1>\n    <p class=\"subtitle\">Real-time subway data system status</p>\n\n    <div class=\"status-banner\">\n      <span class=\"status-icon\">${statusIcon}</span>\n      <span class=\"status-text\">${healthData.status.toUpperCase()}</span>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"grid\">\n      <!-- System Overview -->\n      <div class=\"card\">\n        <div class=\"card-title\">System Overview</div>\n        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Uptime</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${uptimeHours}h ${uptimeMinutes}m</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Cache Hit Rate</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${(healthData.cacheHitRate * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Push Subscriptions</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${healthData.pushSubscriptions}</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Failing Feeds</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\" style=\"color: ${healthData.failingFeedsCount > 0 ? \"#ef4444\" : \"#22c55e\"}\">${healthData.failingFeedsCount}</div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n\n      <!-- Memory Usage -->\n      <div class=\"card\">\n        <div class=\"card-title\">Memory Usage</div>\n        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">RSS (Total)</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${formatBytes(healthData.memory.rssBytes)}</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Heap Used</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${formatBytes(healthData.memory.heapUsedBytes)}</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">of ${formatBytes(healthData.memory.heapTotalBytes)}</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">External</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${formatBytes(healthData.memory.externalBytes)}</div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n\n      <!-- GTFS-RT Feeds -->\n      <div class=\"card\">\n        <div class=\"card-title\">MTA Feeds (GTFS-RT)</div>\n        <table class=\"feed-table\">\n          <thead>\n            <tr>\n              <th>Feed</th>\n              <th>Status</th>\n              <th>Last Success</th>\n              <th>Entities</th>\n              <th>Latency</th>\n              <th>Errors (24h)</th>\n            </tr>\n          </thead>\n          <tbody>\n            ${healthData.feeds\n              .map(\n                (feed) => `\n            <tr>\n              <td>${feed.name}</td>\n              <td><span class=\"status-badge status-${feed.status}\">${feed.status.replace(/_/g, \" \")}</span></td>\n              <td>${timeAgo(feed.lastSuccessAt)}</td>\n              <td>${feed.entityCount}</td>\n              <td>${feed.avgLatencyMs}ms</td>\n              <td>${feed.errorCount24h}</td>\n            </tr>\n          `\n              )\n              .join(\"\")}\n          </tbody>\n        </table>\n      </div>\n\n      <!-- Alerts Status -->\n      <div class=\"card\">\n        <div class=\"card-title\">Service Alerts</div>\n        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Active Alerts</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${healthData.alerts.count}</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Match Rate</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${(healthData.alerts.matchRate * 100).toFixed(0)}%</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">${healthData.alerts.unmatchedCount} unmatched</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Last Update</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${timeAgo(healthData.alerts.lastSuccessAt)}</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Status</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">\n              <span class=\"status-badge status-${healthData.alerts.circuitOpen ? \"circuit_open\" : \"ok\"}\">\n                ${healthData.alerts.circuitOpen ? \"Circuit Open\" : \"OK\"}\n              </span>\n            </div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n\n      <!-- Delay Detection -->\n      <div class=\"card\">\n        <div class=\"card-title\">Delay Detection</div>\n        <div class=\"metric-grid\">\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Detector</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">\n              <span class=\"status-badge status-${healthData.delayDetector.enabled ? \"ok\" : \"never_polled\"}\">\n                ${healthData.delayDetector.enabled ? \"Enabled\" : \"Disabled\"}\n              </span>\n            </div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">${healthData.delayDetector.activePredictions} active predictions</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Predictor</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">\n              <span class=\"status-badge status-${healthData.delayPredictor.enabled ? \"ok\" : \"never_polled\"}\">\n                ${healthData.delayPredictor.enabled ? \"Enabled\" : \"Disabled\"}\n              </span>\n            </div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">${(healthData.delayPredictor.modelAccuracy * 100).toFixed(0)}% accuracy</div>\n          </div>\n          <div class=\"metric\">\n            <div class=\"metric-label\">Equipment</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-value\">${healthData.equipment.outages}</div>\n            <div class=\"metric-sub\">active outages</div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"last-updated\">\n      Last updated: ${new Date(healthData.timestamp).toLocaleString()} •\n      <a href=\"/api/health\" style=\"color: #38bdf8;\">View JSON</a>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n</body>\n</html>`;\n  }\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Health endpoint\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  /**\n   * GET /api/health — Per-feed health status (JSON)\n   *\n   * Query validation: Uses `emptyQuerySchema` (`z.object({}).strict()`).\n   * Any query parameters at all cause a 400 with `{ error: \"validation failed\", details: [...] }`.\n   * No query parameters are accepted.\n   *\n   * Response top-level fields:\n   *   status              (\"ok\" | \"degraded\")\n   *     — \"ok\" when all feeds have circuitOpenAt===null AND lastSuccessAt!==null AND !isStale,\n   *       AND alerts has circuitOpen===false AND lastSuccessAt!==null.\n   *     — \"degraded\" otherwise.\n   *\n   *   timestamp           string (ISO 8601) — new Date().toISOString() at request time.\n   *\n   *   uptime_seconds      number — seconds since SERVER_START_MS (set at module load).\n   *\n   *   feeds               Array<FeedState> — one entry per GTFS-realtime feed.\n   *     Each element:\n   *       id                    string   — feed identifier (e.g. \"1\", \"26\", \"31\", \"36\").\n   *       name                  string   — human-readable feed name.\n   *       status                (\"circuit_open\" | \"never_polled\" | \"stale\" | \"ok\")\n   *         — \"circuit_open\" when circuitOpenAt !== null.\n   *         — \"never_polled\" when lastSuccessAt === null.\n   *         — \"stale\" when isStale === true.\n   *         — \"ok\" otherwise.\n   *       lastSuccessAt         string | null — ISO 8601 of last successful poll, or null.\n   *       lastPollAt            string | null — ISO 8601 of last poll attempt, or null.\n   *       consecutiveFailures  number — count of consecutive failed polls.\n   *       entityCount           number — number of entities in the feed.\n   *       lastError             string | null — truncated (max 100 chars) last error message,\n   *                                  newlines stripped; null if no error.\n   *       tripReplacementPeriod string | null — the feed's trip replacement period.\n   *       avgLatencyMs          number — average of latencyHistory entries.\n   *       errorCount24h         number — count of error timestamps within the last 24 hours.\n   *       parseErrors           (feed-specific type) — parse error details for the feed.\n   *\n   *   alerts              object — alerts polling subsystem status.\n   *     count                  number  — number of alerts tracked.\n   *     lastSuccessAt          number | null — epoch-ms of last successful alert fetch, or null.\n   *     matchRate              number  — fraction of alerts that matched trips (0–1).\n   *     consecutiveFailures    number  — consecutive alert fetch failures.\n   *     circuitOpen            boolean — whether the alerts circuit breaker is open.\n   *     unmatchedCount         number  — alerts that did not match any known trip.\n   *\n   *   delayDetector       object — delay detector subsystem status.\n   *     trackedTrips           number — count of trips currently being tracked.\n   *     activeAlerts           number — count of active predicted delay alerts.\n   *     thresholdMultiplier    number — delay threshold configuration multiplier.\n   *     minTrainsForLineAlert  number — minimum trains needed to trigger a line-level alert.\n   *\n   *   delayPredictor     object — delay predictor subsystem status.\n   *     totalRecords           number — count of delay records stored.\n   *     aggregatedPatterns     number — count of aggregated statistical patterns.\n   *     minObservations        number — minimum observations required for a prediction.\n   *     currentWeather         WeatherCondition — current weather string (e.g. \"clear\").\n   *\n   *   equipment           object (EquipmentCacheStatus) — elevator/escalator outage status.\n   *     lastFetchAt            string | null — ISO 8601 of last fetch attempt.\n   *     lastSuccessAt          string | null — ISO 8601 of last successful fetch.\n   *     outageCount            number — number of stations with tracked outages.\n   *     consecutiveFailures    number — consecutive equipment fetch failures.\n   *     circuitOpen            boolean — whether the equipment circuit breaker is open.\n   *\n   *   pushSubscriptions   number — count of active push notification subscriptions.\n   *\n   *   cacheHitRate        number — fraction 0–1, computed as cache_hits_total /\n   *                            (cache_hits_total + cache_misses_total) summed across all\n   *                            label combinations. Rounded to 2 decimal places.\n   *                            Returns 0 when there have been no cache requests.\n   *\n   *   memory             object — Node.js process memory snapshot via process.memoryUsage().\n   *     rssBytes               number — resident set size in bytes.\n   *     heapUsedBytes          number — active heap usage in bytes.\n   *     heapTotalBytes         number — total heap allocated in bytes.\n   *     externalBytes          number — external (C++/Buffer) memory in bytes.\n   *\n   *   failingFeedsCount  number — count of feeds where consecutiveFailures > 0 AND\n   *                            lastSuccessAt !== null AND time since last success > 5 minutes.\n   *\n   * HTTP status code:\n   *   200 — when fewer than UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD (3) feeds are failing\n   *         (consecutiveFailures > 0, had prior success, last success > 5 min ago).\n   *   503 — when 3 or more feeds meet the failing criteria above.\n   *   Note: \"degraded\" status can coexist with a 200 response (e.g. one stale feed\n   *         but fewer than 3 failing). 503 implies \"degraded\" but not vice versa.\n   */\n  app.get(\"/api/health\", (c) => {\n    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n    const feedStates = getFeedStates();\n    const alertsStatus = getAlertsStatus();\n    const allFeedsOk = feedStates.every(\n      (f) => f.circuitOpenAt === null && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && !f.isStale\n    );\n    const alertsOk = !alertsStatus.circuitOpen && alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt !== null;\n\n    // Count feeds that have been failing for >5 minutes\n    const now = Date.now();\n    const failingFeeds = feedStates.filter(\n      (f) =>\n        f.consecutiveFailures > 0 && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && now - f.lastSuccessAt > 300_000\n    );\n    const unhealthy = failingFeeds.length >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD;\n\n    const status = allFeedsOk && alertsOk ? \"ok\" : \"degraded\";\n    const httpStatus = unhealthy ? 503 : 200;\n\n    const memUsage = process.memoryUsage();\n    const allMetrics = metrics.getAll();\n\n    // Get cache hit rate from metrics (sum across all label combinations)\n    let cacheHitsValue = 0;\n    let cacheMissesValue = 0;\n    const cacheHitsMap = allMetrics.get(\"cache_hits_total\");\n    const cacheMissesMap = allMetrics.get(\"cache_misses_total\");\n    if (cacheHitsMap) {\n      for (const labeled of cacheHitsMap.values()) {\n        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n          cacheHitsValue += labeled.metric.value;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    if (cacheMissesMap) {\n      for (const labeled of cacheMissesMap.values()) {\n        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n          cacheMissesValue += labeled.metric.value;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    const totalCacheRequests = cacheHitsValue + cacheMissesValue;\n    const cacheHitRate =\n      totalCacheRequests > 0 ? Math.round((cacheHitsValue / totalCacheRequests) * 100) / 100 : 0;\n\n    return c.json(\n      {\n        status,\n        timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n        uptime_seconds: Math.floor((Date.now() - SERVER_START_MS) / 1000),\n        feeds: feedStates.map((f) => ({\n          id: f.id,\n          name: f.name,\n          status:\n            f.circuitOpenAt !== null\n              ? \"circuit_open\"\n              : f.lastSuccessAt === null\n                ? \"never_polled\"\n                : f.isStale\n                  ? \"stale\"\n                  : \"ok\",\n          lastSuccessAt: f.lastSuccessAt ? new Date(f.lastSuccessAt).toISOString() : null,\n          lastPollAt: f.lastPollAt ? new Date(f.lastPollAt).toISOString() : null,\n          consecutiveFailures: f.consecutiveFailures,\n          entityCount: f.entityCount,\n          lastError: f.lastErrorMessage\n            ? f.lastErrorMessage.slice(0, 100).replace(/[\\r\\n]/g, \" \")\n            : null,\n          tripReplacementPeriod: f.tripReplacementPeriod,\n          avgLatencyMs: avgLatency(f.latencyHistory),\n          errorCount24h: errorCount24h(f.errorTimestamps),\n          parseErrors: f.parseErrors,\n        })),\n        alerts: {\n          count: alertsStatus.alertCount,\n          lastSuccessAt: alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt,\n          matchRate: alertsStatus.matchRate,\n          consecutiveFailures: alertsStatus.consecutiveFailures,\n          circuitOpen: alertsStatus.circuitOpen,\n          unmatchedCount: alertsStatus.unmatchedCount,\n        },\n        delayDetector: getDelayDetectorStatus(),\n        delayPredictor: getDelayPredictorStatus(),\n        equipment: getEquipmentStatus(),\n        pushSubscriptions: getSubscriptionCount(),\n        cacheHitRate,\n        memory: {\n          rssBytes: memUsage.rss,\n          heapUsedBytes: memUsage.heapUsed,\n          heapTotalBytes: memUsage.heapTotal,\n          externalBytes: memUsage.external,\n        },\n        failingFeedsCount: failingFeeds.length,\n      },\n      httpStatus as 200 | 503\n    );\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Public status page (human-readable dashboard)\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/status\", (c) => {\n    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    // Fetch the same data as the health endpoint\n    const feedStates = getFeedStates();\n    const alertsStatus = getAlertsStatus();\n    const allFeedsOk = feedStates.every(\n      (f) => f.circuitOpenAt === null && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && !f.isStale\n    );\n    const alertsOk = !alertsStatus.circuitOpen && alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt !== null;\n\n    // Count feeds that have been failing for >5 minutes\n    const now = Date.now();\n    const failingFeeds = feedStates.filter(\n      (f) =>\n        f.consecutiveFailures > 0 && f.lastSuccessAt !== null && now - f.lastSuccessAt > 300_000\n    );\n    const unhealthy = failingFeeds.length >= UNHEALTHY_FEED_THRESHOLD;\n\n    const status = allFeedsOk && alertsOk ? \"ok\" : \"degraded\";\n    const httpStatus = unhealthy ? 503 : 200;\n\n    const memUsage = process.memoryUsage();\n    const allMetrics = metrics.getAll();\n\n    // Get cache hit rate from metrics\n    let cacheHitsValue = 0;\n    let cacheMissesValue = 0;\n    const cacheHitsMap = allMetrics.get(\"cache_hits_total\");\n    const cacheMissesMap = allMetrics.get(\"cache_misses_total\");\n    if (cacheHitsMap) {\n      for (const labeled of cacheHitsMap.values()) {\n        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n          cacheHitsValue += labeled.metric.value;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    if (cacheMissesMap) {\n      for (const labeled of cacheMissesMap.values()) {\n        if (labeled.metric.type === \"counter\") {\n          cacheMissesValue += labeled.metric.value;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    const totalCacheRequests = cacheHitsValue + cacheMissesValue;\n    const cacheHitRate =\n      totalCacheRequests > 0 ? Math.round((cacheHitsValue / totalCacheRequests) * 100) / 100 : 0;\n\n    const healthData = {\n      status,\n      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),\n      uptime_seconds: Math.floor((Date.now() - SERVER_START_MS) / 1000),\n      feeds: feedStates.map((f) => ({\n        id: f.id,\n        name: f.name,\n        status:\n          f.circuitOpenAt !== null\n            ? \"circuit_open\"\n            : f.lastSuccessAt === null\n              ? \"never_polled\"\n              : f.isStale\n                ? \"stale\"\n                : \"ok\",\n        lastSuccessAt: f.lastSuccessAt ? new Date(f.lastSuccessAt).toISOString() : null,\n        lastPollAt: f.lastPollAt ? new Date(f.lastPollAt).toISOString() : null,\n        consecutiveFailures: f.consecutiveFailures,\n        entityCount: f.entityCount,\n        lastError: f.lastErrorMessage\n          ? f.lastErrorMessage.slice(0, 100).replace(/[\\r\\n]/g, \" \")\n          : null,\n        avgLatencyMs: avgLatency(f.latencyHistory),\n        errorCount24h: errorCount24h(f.errorTimestamps),\n      })),\n      alerts: {\n        count: alertsStatus.alertCount,\n        lastSuccessAt: alertsStatus.lastSuccessAt,\n        matchRate: alertsStatus.matchRate,\n        consecutiveFailures: alertsStatus.consecutiveFailures,\n        circuitOpen: alertsStatus.circuitOpen,\n        unmatchedCount: alertsStatus.unmatchedCount,\n      },\n      delayDetector: getDelayDetectorStatus(),\n      delayPredictor: getDelayPredictorStatus(),\n      equipment: getEquipmentStatus(),\n      pushSubscriptions: getSubscriptionCount(),\n      cacheHitRate,\n      memory: {\n        rssBytes: memUsage.rss,\n        heapUsedBytes: memUsage.heapUsed,\n        heapTotalBytes: memUsage.heapTotal,\n        externalBytes: memUsage.external,\n      },\n      failingFeedsCount: failingFeeds.length,\n    };\n\n    const html = buildStatusHtml(healthData);\n    c.header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/html; charset=utf-8\");\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", \"public, max-age=30\");\n    return c.html(html, httpStatus as 200 | 503);\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Metrics export endpoint (for Prometheus)\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/api/metrics\", (c) => {\n    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    const metricsText = metrics.exportPrometheus();\n    c.header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain; version=0.0.4; charset=utf-8\");\n    return c.text(metricsText);\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // CSP violation reporting endpoint\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.post(\"/api/security/csp-report\", async (c) => {\n    try {\n      const contentType = c.req.header(\"Content-Type\") ?? \"\";\n      const baseContentType = contentType.split(\";\")[0]!.trim().toLowerCase();\n      const allowed = [\"application/csp-report\", \"application/json\", \"application/reports+json\"];\n      if (!allowed.includes(baseContentType)) {\n        return c.json({ error: \"Unsupported Media Type\" }, 415);\n      }\n\n      const report = await c.req.json().catch(() => null);\n\n      if (!report) {\n        return c.json({ error: \"Invalid report\" }, 400);\n      }\n\n      // Log the CSP violation for security monitoring\n      logger.warn(\"CSP violation detected\", {\n        \"user-agent\": c.req.header(\"user-agent\"),\n        referrer: c.req.header(\"referrer\"),\n        \"x-forwarded-for\": c.req.header(\"x-forwarded-for\")?.split(\",\")[0] ?? \"unknown\",\n        report,\n      });\n\n      // Return 200 to acknowledge the report\n      return c.json({ received: true });\n    } catch (error) {\n      logger.error(\"Failed to process CSP report\", error as Error);\n      return c.json({ error: \"Failed to process report\" }, 400);\n    }\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Security disclosure policy (RFC 9116)\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/.well-known/security.txt\", (c) => {\n    const contact = process.env[\"SECURITY_CONTACT\"] ?? \"mailto:security@jedarden.com\";\n    const body =\n      `Contact: ${contact}\\n` +\n      `Preferred-Languages: en\\n` +\n      `Policy: https://github.com/jedarden/mta-my-way/security/policy\\n`;\n    c.header(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain; charset=utf-8\");\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", \"max-age=86400\");\n    return c.text(body);\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Real-time arrivals\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/api/arrivals/:id\", (c) => {\n    const params = validateParams(c, stationIdParamsSchema);\n    if (params instanceof Response) return params;\n\n    const { id: stationId } = params;\n    const arrivals = getArrivals(stationId);\n\n    if (!arrivals) {\n      return c.json({ error: \"Station not found or app.get(\"/api/stations\", (c) => {\n    // Validate that no unexpected query parameters are passed\n    const query = validateQuery(c, emptyQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", STATIC_CACHE_HEADER);\n    return c.json(Object.values(stations));\n  });\n\n  app.get(\"/api/stations/search\", (c) => {\n    const startTime = Date.now();\n    const query = validateQuery(c, stationSearchQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    const { q } = query;\n    const trimmedQuery = q.trim();\n    const normalizedQuery = normalizeForSearch(trimmedQuery);\n\n    const results = Object.values(stations)\n      .filter((station) => stationMatchesQuery(station, normalizedQuery, trimmedQuery))\n      .map((station) => ({\n        station,\n        score: scoreSearchResult(station, trimmedQuery),\n      }))\n      .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)\n      .map(({ station }) => station);\n\n    const duration = (Date.now() - startTime) / 1000;\n    recordStationSearchDuration(duration);\n    recordStationSearchRequest(results.length);\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", STATIC_CACHE_HEADER);\n    return c.json(results);\n  });\n\n  app.get(\"/api/stations/:id\", (c) => {\n    const params = validateParams(c, stationIdParamsSchema);\n    if (params instanceof Response) return params;\n\n    const { id } = params;\n    const station = stations[id];\n\n    if (!station) {\n      return c.json({ error: \"Station not found\" }, 404);\n    }\n\n    const complex = stationToComplex.get(id);\n\n    const response: Record<string, unknown> = {\n      ...station,\n      complexStations: [] as Station[],\n      complexLines: station.lines,\n    };\n\n    if (complex) {\n      const complexStations = complex.stations\n        .map((sid) => stations[sid])\n        .filter((s): s is Station => s !== undefined);\n\n      const allLines = new Set<string>();\n      for (const s of complexStations) {\n        for (const line of s.lines) {\n          allLines.add(line);\n        }\n      }\n\n      response.complexId = complex.complexId;\n      response.complexName = complex.name;\n      response.complexStations = complexStations;\n      response.complexLines = Array.from(allLines).sort();\n    }\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", STATIC_CACHE_HEADER);\n    return c.json(response);\n  });\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // GTFS static route data\n  // requirePermission(\"commutes:create\" as Permission),\n    auditLogAccess(\"commute\", \"create\"),\n    async (c) => {\n      const startTime = Date.now();\n      let success = false;\n      let hasTransfers = false;\n      const accessibleMode = false;\n\n      try {\n        const body = await validateBody(c, commuteAnalyzeRequestSchema);\n        if (body instanceof Response) return body;\n\n        const {\n          originId,\n          destinationId,\n          preferredLines = [],\n          commuteId = \"default\",\n          accessibleMode: accessible = false,\n        } = body;\n\n        const accessibleMode = accessible;\n\n        if (!stations[originId]) {\n          return c.json({ error: `Origin station not found: ${originId}` }, 404);\n        }\n        if (!stations[destinationId]) {\n          return c.json({ error: `Destination station not found: ${destinationId}` }, 404);\n        }\n\n        const analysis = transferEngine.analyzeCommute(\n success = true;\n\n        const duration = (Date.now() - startTime) / 1000;\n        recordCommuteAnalysisDuration(duration);\n        recordCommuteAnalysisRequest(success, hasTransfers, accessibleMode);\n\n        c.header(\"Cache-Control\", `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTLS.api}`);\n        return c.json(analysis);\n      } catch (error) {\n        logger.error(\"Commute analysis error\", error instanceof Error ? error : undefined);\n\n        const duration = (Date.now() - startTime) / 1000;\n        recordCommuteAnalysisDuration(duration);\n        recordCommuteAnalysisRequest(false, false, accessibleMode);\n\n        return c.json({ error: \"Failed to analyze commute\" }, 500);\n      }\n    }\n  );\n\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Alerts\n  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------\n  app.get(\"/api/alerts\", (c) => {\n    // Validate query parameters to prevent injection attacks\n    const query = validateQuery(c, alertsQuerySchema);\n    if (query instanceof Response) return query;\n\n    // Apply filtering if query parameters are provided\n    let officialAlerts = getAllAlerts();\n    if (query.lineId) {\n      officialAlerts = getAlertsForLine(query.lineId);\n    }\n    if (query.activeOnly) {\n      const now = Date.now() / 1000; // Convert to seconds for comparison\n      officialAlerts = officialAlerts.filter((a) => {\n        const start = a.activePeriod.start;\n        const end = a.activePeriod.end;\n        // Alert is active if we're past the start time and either no end or before end\n        return now >= start && (!end || now <= end);\n      });\n    }\n\n    const predictedAlerts = getPredictedAlerts();\n    const status = getAlertsStatus();\n    const delayDetector = getDelayDetectorStatus();\n\n    // Merge official + predicted alerts; predicted are tagged with source field\n    const alerts = [...officialAlerts, ...predictedAlerts];\n\n    c.header(\"Cache-Control\", `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTLS.api}`);\n    return c.json({\n      alerts,\n      meta: {\n        count: alerts.length,\n        officialCount: officialAlerts.length,\n        predictedCount: predictedAlerts.length,\n        lastUpdatedAt: status.lastSuccessAt,\n        matchRate: status.matchRate,\n        consecutiveFailures: status.consecutiveFailures,\n        circuitOpen: status.circuitOpen,\n        delayDetector,\n      },\n    });\n  });\n\n auditLogAccess(\"subscription\", \"create\"),\n    async (c) => {\n      try {\n        const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n        const body = await validateBody(c, pushSubscribeRequestSchema);\n        if (body instanceof Response) return body;\n\n        // Use the authenticated user's keyId as the owner\n        const ownerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n        upsertSubscription(body, ownerId);\n\n        logger.info(\"Push subscription registered\", {\n          lines: body.favorites?.map((f) => f.lines).flat() ?? [],\n          total_subscriptions: getSubscriptionCount(),\n          ownerId,\n        });\n\n        return c.json({ success: true });\n      } catch (err) {\n        logger.error(\"Push app.delete(\n    \"/api/push/unsubscribe\",\n    requireOwnershipOrAdmin(\"subscriptions\", {\n      getOwnerId: async (c) => {\n        const body = await c.req.json().catch(() => ({}));\n        return getSubscriptionOwner(body.endpoint) || \"\";\n      },\n      adminBypass: true,\n    }),\n    requireResourceAccess(\"subscription\", \"delete\", { adminBypass: true }),\n    auditLogAccess(\"subscription\", \"delete\"),\n    async (c) => {\n      try {\n        const auth = getRbacAuthContext(c);\n        const body = await validateBody(c, pushUnsubscribeRequestSchema);\n        if (body instanceof Response) return body;\n\n        // Use the authenticated user's keyId as the owner\n        const ownerId = auth?.keyId || \"anonymous\";\n        const removed = removeSubscription(body.endpoint, ownerId);\n\n        logger.info(\"Push subscription removed\", {\n          removed,\n          total_subscriptions: getSubscriptionCount(),\n          ownerId,\n        });\n\n        return c.json({ success: true });\n      } catch (err) {\n        logger.error(\"Push subscription removal failed\", err as Error);\n        return c.json({ error: \"Failed to * Zod validation helpers for Hono route handlers.\n *\n * Provides validation for:\n * - Request bodies (JSON)\n * - Query parameters\n * - Path parameters\n *\n * Returns 400 with structured error details on validation failure.\n */\n\nimport type { Context } from \"hono\";\nimport type { ZodError, ZodSchema } from \"zod\";\nimport { ZodIssueCode } from \"zod\";\nimport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\nimport { sanitizeObject, sanitizeStringSimple } from \"./sanitization.js\";\n\ninterface ValidationErrorDetail {\n  field: string;\n  message: string;\n}\n\ninterface ValidationErrorResponse {\n  error: \"validation failed\";\n  details: ValidationErrorDetail[];\n}\n\nfunction formatZodError(error: ZodError): ValidationErrorDetail[] {\n  return error.issues.map((issue) => {\n    // For nested objects, join the path with dots\n    const field = issue.path.map((p) => (typeof p === \"number\" ? `[${p}]` : p)).join(\".\");\n\n    if (issue.code === ZodIssueCode.invalid_enum_value) {\n      return {\n        field,\n        message: `Must be one of: ${issue.options.join(\", \")}`,\n      };\n    }\n\n    if (issue.code === ZodIssueCode.too_small) {\n      if (issue.type === \"string\") {\n        return {\n          field,\n          message: issue.message || `String must be at least ${issue.minimum} character(s)`,\n        };\n      }\n      if (issue.type === \"array\") {\n        return {\n          field,\n          message: issue.message || `Array must contain at least ${issue.minimum} item(s)`,\n        };\n      }\n      if (issue.type === \"number\") {\n        return { field, message: issue.message || `Number must be at least ${issue.minimum}` };\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (issue.code === ZodIssueCode.too_big) {\n      if (issue.type === \"string\") {\n        return {\n          field,\n          message: issue.message || `String must be at most ${issue.maximum} character(s)`,\n        };\n      }\n      if (issue.type === \"array\") {\n        return {\n          field,\n          message: issue.message || `Array must contain at most ${issue.maximum} item(s)`,\n        };\n      }\n      if (issue.type === \"number\") {\n        return { field, message: issue.message || `Number must be at most ${issue.maximum}` };\n      }\n    }\n\n    return { field, message: issue.message || `Invalid value` };\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse and validate a JSON request body against a Zod schema.\n * Returns the validated data on success, or a Response on failure.\n * Caller must check `if (body instanceof Response) return body;`\n *\n * Sanitizes all string inputs to prevent XSS, SQL injection, and other attacks.\n */\nexport async function validateBody<T>(c: Context, schema: ZodSchema<T>): Promise<T | Response> {\n  let body: unknown;\n  try {\n    body = await c.req.json();\n  } catch {\n    const errorResponse: ValidationErrorResponse = {\n      error: \"validation failed\",\n      details: [{ field: \"\", message: \"Request body must be valid JSON\" }],\n    };\n    logger.warn(\"Request body validation failed: invalid JSON\", {\n      path: c.req.path,\n      method: c.req.method,\n    });\n    return c.json(errorResponse, 400);\n  }\n\n  // Sanitize string fields in the body before validation\n  // This prevents XSS, SQL injection, and other injection attacks\n  body = sanitizeObject(body);\n\n  const result = schema.safeParse(body);\n\n  if (!result.success) {\n    const errorResponse: ValidationErrorResponse = {\n      error: \"validation failed\",\n      details: formatZodError(result.error),\n    };\n    logger.warn(\"Request body validation failed\", {\n      path: c.req.path,\n      method: c.req.method,\n      errors: formatZodError(result.error),\n    });\n    return c.json(errorResponse, 400);\n  }\n\n  return result.data;\n}\n\n/**\n * Sanitize path parameters to prevent injection attacks.\n *\n * Path parameters can be exploited for:\n * - Path traversal attacks (../../)\n * - Command injection\n * - XSS via reflected parameters\n *\n * This function sanitizes path parameter values before they reach\n * the route handler, providing defense in depth.\n */\nfunction sanitizePathParams(params: Record<string, string>): Record<string, string> {\n  const sanitized: Record<string, string> = {};\n  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(params)) {\n    // Sanitize the key as well\n    const sanitizedKey = sanitizeStringSimple(key, {\n      normalizeWhitespace: false,\n      maxLength: 100,\n    });\n\n    // Sanitize the value - focus on path traversal and command injection\n    // for path params since they're typically IDs or enum values\n    sanitized[sanitizedKey] = sanitizeStringSimple(value, {\n      stripHtml: true,\n      preventSqlInjection: true,\n      preventCommandInjection: true,\n      preventPathTraversal: true,\n      normalizeWhitespace: false, // Preserve ID formatting\n      maxLength: 200, // Generous limit for path params\n    });\n  }\n  return sanitized;\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate query parameters against a Zod schema.\n * Returns the validated data on success, or a Response on failure.\n * Caller must check `if (query instanceof Response) return query;`\n *\n * Uses sanitized query parameters from the inputSanitization middleware\n * if available, providing defense in depth against injection attacks.\n */\nexport function validateQuery<T>(c: Context, schema: ZodSchema<T>): T | Response {\n  // Prefer sanitized query parameters from the inputSanitization middleware\n  // This provides defense in depth - sanitization happens before validation\n  const sanitizedQuery = (c.get(\"sanitizedQuery\") as Record<string, string>) || undefined;\n\n  // Fall back to raw query params if sanitization hasn't run\n  const queryParams = sanitizedQuery || c.req.query();\n\n  const result = schema.safeParse(queryParams);\n  if (!result.success) {\n    const errorResponse: ValidationErrorResponse = {\n      error: \"validation failed\",\n      details: formatZodError(result.error),\n    };\n    logger.warn(\"Query parameter validation failed\", {\n      path: c.req.path,\n      method: c.req.method,\n      errors: formatZodError(result.error),\n    });\n    return c.json(errorResponse, 400);\n  }\n  return result.data;\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate path parameters against a Zod schema.\n * Returns the validated data on success, or a Response on failure.\n * Caller must check `if (params instanceof Response) return params;`\n *\n * Sanitizes path parameters to prevent injection attacks including\n * path traversal, command injection, and reflected XSS.\n */\nexport function validateParams<T>(c: Context, schema: ZodSchema<T>): T | Response {\n  const pathParams = c.req.param();\n\n  // Sanitize path parameters before validation\n  const sanitizedParams = sanitizePathParams(pathParams);\n\n  const result = schema.safeParse(sanitizedParams);\n  if (!result.success) {\n    const errorResponse: ValidationErrorResponse = {\n      error: \"validation failed\",\n      details: formatZodError(result.error),\n    };\n    logger.warn(\"Path parameter validation failed\", {\n      path: c.req.path,\n      method: c.req.method,\n      errors: formatZodError(result.error),\n    });\n    return c.json(errorResponse, 400);\n  }\n  return result.data;\n}\n","numLines":220,"startLine":1,"totalLines":220}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_4761e1174d5b4d86a9f9553c","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/**\n2\t * Shared sanitization utilities for input validation.\n3\t *\n4\t * Provides centralized sanitization functions to prevent:\n5\t * - XSS attacks (HTML tags, event handlers)\n6\t * - SQL injection attacks\n7\t * - Path traversal attacks\n8\t * - Command injection attacks\n9\t */\n10\t\n11\timport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\n12\t\n13\t/**\n14\t * Script and style tag pattern - removes these tags AND their content.\n15\t * This prevents XSS by removing JavaScript and CSS entirely.\n16\t */\n17\tconst SCRIPT_STYLE_PATTERN = /<(script|style)[^>]*>.*?<\\/\\1[^>]*>/gi;\n18\t\n19\t/**\n20\t * HTML tag pattern for stripping (without content).\n21\t * Matches HTML tags but preserves the content between them.\n22\t */\n23\tconst HTML_TAG_PATTERN = /<[^>]*>/gi;\n24\t\n25\t/**\n26\t * Self-closing HTML tag pattern.\n27\t * Matches self-closing tags like <br />, <img />, etc.\n28\t */\n29\t// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\n30\tconst SELFCLOSING_TAG_PATTERN = /<[^>]*\\/>/gi;\n31\t\n32\t/**\n33\t * JavaScript event handler pattern.\n34\t * Matches on* attributes that could execute JavaScript.\n35\t */\n36\tconst EVENT_HANDLER_PATTERN = /on\\w+\\s*=/gi;\n37\t\n38\t/**\n39\t * JavaScript protocol pattern for URLs.\n40\t * Matches javascript: pseudo-protocol that can execute code.\n41\t */\n42\tconst JAVASCRIPT_PROTOCOL_PATTERN = /javascript:/gi;\n43\t\n44\t/**\n45\t * JavaScript function call pattern.\n46\t * Matches common JavaScript function calls that could execute code.\n47\t * Captures the function name, opening paren, content, and closing paren.\n48\t * Excludes \"script\" as a keyword to avoid false positives with HTML encoded entities\n49\t */\n50\tconst JAVASCRIPT_FUNCTION_PATTERN =\n51\t  /\\b(alert|confirm|prompt|eval|exec|setTimeout|setInterval|Function|document\\.write|location\\.|window\\.|\\.innerHTML|\\.outerHTML)\\s*\\([^)]*\\)/gi;\n52\t\n53\t/**\n54\t * SQL injection pattern - more specific to avoid false positives.\n55\t *\n56\t * Detects:\n57\t * - Tautological conditions (1=1, 'a'='a')\n58\t * - Comment sequences (--, slash-star, star-slash)\n59\t * - UNION-based injections\n60\t * - Stacked queries (semicolon)\n61\t * - Boolean-based injection with quoted strings\n62\t * - Time-based injection patterns\n63\t *\n64\t * Uses word boundaries and more specific patterns to reduce false positives\n65\t * on legitimate text containing \"and\", \"or\", etc.\n66\t */\n67\tconst SQL_INJECTION_PATTERN =\n68\t  // eslint-disable-next-line no-useless-escape\n69\t  /(\\bOR\\b\\s+\\d+\\s*=\\s*\\d+)|(\\bAND\\b\\s+\\d+\\s*=\\s*\\d+)|('\\s*\\|\\|?\\s*')|('\\s*(OR|AND)\\s+'[^']+'\\s*=\\s*'[^']*')|('\\s*;)|(\\bor\\s+\\d+\\s*=\\s*\\d+\\b)|(\\band\\s+\\d+\\s*=\\s*\\d+\\b)|(\\bunion\\b\\s+\\bselect\\b)|(\\/\\*)|(\\*\\/)|(\\-\\-)|(;)|(waitfor\\s+delay)|(\\bsleep\\s*\\()/gi;\n70\t\n71\t/**\n72\t * Command injection patterns.\n73\t * Detects shell metacharacters and command chaining.\n74\t * Note: $ is excluded here to avoid false positives with NoSQL operators like $ne\n75\t */\n76\tconst COMMAND_INJECTION_PATTERN = /[;&|`()<>]/g;\n77\t\n78\t/**\n79\t * Path traversal patterns.\n80\t * Detects attempts to access parent directories.\n81\t */\n82\tconst PATH_TRAVERSAL_PATTERN = /\\.\\.[/\\\\]/g;\n83\t\n84\t/**\n85\t * LDAP injection patterns.\n86\t * Detects attempts to inject LDAP queries.\n87\t */\n88\tconst LDAP_INJECTION_PATTERN = /(\\(|\\)|\\*|\\||&|\\||!=|>=|<=|=\\*|\\()/gi;\n89\t\n90\t/**\n91\t * NoSQL injection patterns.\n92\t * Detects attempts to inject NoSQL queries (MongoDB, etc.).\n93\t */\n94\tconst NOSQL_INJECTION_PATTERN = /(\\$where|\\$ne|\\$gt|\\$lt|\\$in|\\$or|\\$and|\\$regex|\\$expr)/gi;\n95\t\n96\t/**\n97\t * XSS patterns beyond HTML tags.\n98\t * Detects encoded and obfuscated XSS attempts.\n99\t */\n100\tconst XSS_PATTERN =\n101\t  /(%3C|&lt;|&#60;|&#x3C;|%3E|&gt;|&#62;|&#x3E;|%22|&quot;|&#34;|&#x22;|%27|&apos;|&#39;|&#x27;)/gi;\n102\t\n103\t/**\n104\t * Template injection patterns.\n105\t * Detects attempts to inject template engine syntax.\n106\t */\n107\t\n108\t// eslint-disable-next-line no-useless-escape\n109\tconst TEMPLATE_INJECTION_PATTERN = /(\\{\\{|\\}\\}|\\$\\{|\\#{|@{{)/gi;\n110\t\n111\t/**\n112\t * Log injection / log forging patterns.\n113\t * Detects attempts to inject log entries with CRLF sequences.\n114\t */\n115\tconst LOG_INJECTION_PATTERN = /[\\r\\n]\\s*(ERROR|WARN|INFO|DEBUG|TRACE)/gi;\n116\t\n117\t/**\n118\t * Sanitization options.\n119\t */\n120\texport interface SanitizationOptions {\n121\t  /** Strip HTML tags from string inputs */\n122\t  stripHtml?: boolean;\n123\t  /** Remove potential SQL injection patterns */\n124\t  preventSqlInjection?: boolean;\n125\t  /** Normalize whitespace (collapse multiple spaces) */\n126\t  normalizeWhitespace?: boolean;\n127\t  /** Remove command injection patterns */\n128\t  preventCommandInjection?: boolean;\n129\t  /** Remove path traversal patterns */\n130\t  preventPathTraversal?: boolean;\n131\t  /** Maximum string length (0 = no limit) */\n132\t  maxLength?: number;\n133\t  /** Remove LDAP injection patterns */\n134\t  preventLdapInjection?: boolean;\n135\t  /** Remove NoSQL injection patterns */\n136\t  preventNosqlInjection?: boolean;\n137\t  /** Remove XSS patterns including encoded variants */\n138\t  preventXss?: boolean;\n139\t  /** Remove template injection patterns */\n140\t  preventTemplateInjection?: boolean;\n141\t  /** Remove log injection patterns */\n142\t  preventLogInjection?: boolean;\n143\t  /** Preserve case (default: false - converts to lowercase) */\n144\t  preserveCase?: boolean;\n145\t  /** Preserve whitespace (default: false - normalizes) */\n146\t  preserveWhitespace?: boolean;\n147\t}\n148\t\n149\t/**\n150\t * Default sanitization options.\n151\t */\n152\tconst DEFAULT_OPTIONS: SanitizationOptions = {\n153\t  stripHtml: true,\n154\t  preventSqlInjection: true,\n155\t  preventCommandInjection: true,\n156\t  preventPathTraversal: true,\n157\t  normalizeWhitespace: true,\n158\t  maxLength: 10000,\n159\t  preventLdapInjection: true,\n160\t  preventNosqlInjection: true,\n161\t  preventXss: true,\n162\t  preventTemplateInjection: true,\n163\t  preventLogInjection: true,\n164\t  preserveCase: false,\n165\t  preserveWhitespace: false,\n166\t};\n167\t\n168\t/**\n169\t * Sanitization result with metadata.\n170\t */\n171\texport interface SanitizationResult {\n172\t  /** The sanitized value */\n173\t  value: string;\n174\t  /** Whether any sanitization was performed */\n175\t  wasSanitized: boolean;\n176\t  /** The type of sanitization performed (if any) */\n177\t  sanitizationType?: string[];\n178\t}\n179\t\n180\t/**\n181\t * Sanitize a string value.\n182\t *\n183\t * Returns both the sanitized value and metadata about what was sanitized.\n184\t * This is useful for logging and security monitoring.\n185\t *\n186\t * IMPORTANT: Pattern detection happens on the CURRENT value at each step,\n187\t * which means patterns detected earlier may be removed by later sanitizations.\n188\t * This is intentional to log all threats present in the original input.\n189\t */\n190\texport function sanitizeString(\n191\t  input: string,\n192\t  options: SanitizationOptions = {}\n193\t): SanitizationResult {\n194\t  const mergedOptions = { ...DEFAULT_OPTIONS, ...options };\n195\t  const sanitizationTypes: string[] = [];\n196\t  let sanitized = input;\n197\t\n198\t  // Apply length limit first\n199\t  if (mergedOptions.maxLength && mergedOptions.maxLength > 0) {\n200\t    if (sanitized.length > mergedOptions.maxLength) {\n201\t      sanitizationTypes.push(\"length_limit\");\n202\t      sanitized = sanitized.slice(0, mergedOptions.maxLength);\n203\t      logger.warn(\"Input exceeded maximum length, truncated\", {\n204\t        originalLength: input.length,\n205\t        maxLength: mergedOptions.maxLength,\n206\t        preview: input.slice(0, 50),\n207\t      });\n208\t    }\n209\t  }\n210\t\n211\t  // IMPORTANT: Detect ALL patterns first on the current sanitized value\n212\t  // BEFORE any replacements that might remove the patterns.\n213\t  // This ensures we log all threats present in the input.\n214\t\n215\t  // Detect SQL injection patterns\n216\t  if (mergedOptions.preventSqlInjection && SQL_INJECTION_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {\n217\t    sanitizationTypes.push(\"sql_injection\");\n218\t    logger.warn(\"SQL injection pattern detected in input\", {\n219\t      preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n220\t    });\n221\t  }\n222\t\n223\t  // Detect command injection patterns\n224\t  if (mergedOptions.preventCommandInjection && COMMAND_INJECTION_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {\n225\t    sanitizationTypes.push(\"command_injection\");\n226\t    logger.warn(\"Command injection pattern detected in input\", {\n227\t      preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n228\t    });\n229\t  }\n230\t\n231\t  // Detect path traversal patterns\n232\t  if (mergedOptions.preventPathTraversal && PATH_TRAVERSAL_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {\n233\t    sanitizationTypes.push(\"path_traversal\");\n234\t    logger.warn(\"Path traversal pattern detected in input\", {\n235\t      preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n236\t    });\n237\t  }\n238\t\n239\t  // Detect LDAP injection patterns\n240\t  if (mergedOptions.preventLdapInjection && LDAP_INJECTION_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {\n241\t    sanitizationTypes.push(\"ldap_injection\");\n242\t    logger.warn(\"LDAP injection pattern detected in input\", {\n243\t      preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n244\t    });\n245\t  }\n246\t\n247\t  // Detect NoSQL injection patterns\n248\t  if (mergedOptions.preventNosqlInjection && NOSQL_INJECTION_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {\n249\t    sanitizationTypes.push(\"nosql_injection\");\n250\t    logger.warn(\"NoSQL injection pattern detected in \"\");\n308\t\n309\t    // Remove event handlers\n310\t    const eventHandlerMatch = sanitized.match(EVENT_HANDLER_PATTERN);\n311\t    if (eventHandlerMatch) {\n312\t      if (!sanitizationTypes.includes(\"html_tags\")) {\n313\t        sanitizationTypes.push(\"event_handlers\");\n314\t      }\n315\t      logger.warn(\"Event handlers detected and removed from input\", {\n316\t        pattern: eventHandlerMatch[0],\n317\t        preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n318\t      });\n319\t    }\n320\t    sanitized = sanitized.replace(EVENT_HANDLER_PATTERN, \"\");\n321\t\n322\t    // Remove javascript: protocol\n323\t    const jsProtocolMatch = sanitized.match(JAVASCRIPT_PROTOCOL_PATTERN);\n324\t    if (jsProtocolMatch) {\n325\t      if function calls\n335\t    const jsFunctionMatch = sanitized.match(JAVASCRIPT_FUNCTION_PATTERN);\n336\t    if (jsFunctionMatch) {\n337\t      if (!sanitizationTypes.includes(\"html_tags\")) {\n338\t        sanitizationTypes.push(\"javascript_function\");\n339\t      }\n340\t      logger.warn(\"JavaScript function call detected and removed from input\", {\n341\t        pattern: sanitizationTypes.push(\"sql_injection\");\n360\t    }\n361\t  }\n362\t\n363\t  // Apply command injection sanitization\n364\t  if (mergedOptions.preventCommandInjection) {\n365\t    sanitized = \").trim();\n460\t  }\n461\t\n462\t  return sanitized;\n463\t}\n464\t\n465\t/**\n466\t * Recursively sanitize string values in an object.\n467\t *\n468\t * Handles:\n469\t * - Primitive strings\n470\t * - Nested objects\n471\t * - Arrays\n472\t * - null and undefined values\n473\t */\n474\texport function sanitizeObject(obj: unknown, options: SanitizationOptions = {}): unknown {\n475\t  if (typeof }\n482\t\n483\t  if (obj !== null && typeof obj === \"object\") {\n484\t    const sanitized: Record<string, unknown> = {};\n485\t    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(obj)) {\n486\t      // obj;\n496\t}\n497\t\n498\t/**\n499\t * Sanitize query parameters.\n500\t *\n501\t * Handles both single values and arrays.\n502\t */\n503\texport function sanitizeParams(\n504\t  params: /^hmac_sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}$/i.test(signature);\n555\t}\n556\t\n557\t/**\n558\t * Encode output for HTML context to prevent XSS.\n559\t * Escapes <, >, &, \", and ' characters.\n560\t */\n561\texport .replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\")\n567\t    .replace(/'/g, \"&#39;\");\n568\t}\n569\t\n570\t/**\n571\t * Encode output for JavaScript string context to prevent XSS.\n572\t * Escapes backslashes, quotes, and other special characters.\n573\t */\n574\texport function encodeJsOutput(unsafe: string): string {\n575\t  return unsafe\n576\t    .replace(/\\\\/g, \"\\\\\\\\\")\n577\t    .replace(/'/g, \"\\\\'\")\n578\t    .replace(/\"/g, '\\\\\"')\n579\t    .replace(/\\n/g, \"\\\\n\")\n580\t    .replace(/\\r/g, \"\\\\r\")\n581\t    .replace(/\\t/g, \"\\\\t\")\n582\t    .replace(/\\f/g, \"\\\\f\")\n583\t    .replace(/\\v/g, \"\\\\v\")\n584\t    .replace(/\\0/g, \"\\\\0\");\n585\t}\n586\t\n587\t/**\n588\t * Encode output for URL parameter context.\n589\t */\n590\texport function * Shared sanitization utilities for input validation.\n *\n * Provides centralized sanitization functions to prevent:\n * - XSS attacks (HTML tags, event handlers)\n * - SQL injection attacks\n * - Path traversal attacks\n * - Command injection attacks\n */\n\nimport { logger } from \"../observability/logger.js\";\n\n/**\n * Script and style tag pattern - removes these tags AND their content.\n * This prevents XSS by removing JavaScript and CSS entirely.\n */\nconst SCRIPT_STYLE_PATTERN = /<(script|style)[^>]*>.*?<\\/\\1[^>]*>/gi;\n\n/**\n * HTML tag pattern for stripping (without content).\n * Matches HTML tags but preserves the content between them.\n */\nconst HTML_TAG_PATTERN = /<[^>]*>/gi;\n\n/**\n * Self-closing HTML tag pattern.\n * Matches self-closing tags like <br />, <img />, etc.\n */\n// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars\nconst SELFCLOSING_TAG_PATTERN = /<[^>]*\\/>/gi;\n\n/**\n * JavaScript event handler pattern.\n * Matches on* attributes that could execute JavaScript.\n */\nconst EVENT_HANDLER_PATTERN = /on\\w+\\s*=/gi;\n\n/**\n * JavaScript protocol pattern for URLs.\n * Matches javascript: pseudo-protocol that can execute code.\n */\nconst JAVASCRIPT_PROTOCOL_PATTERN = /javascript:/gi;\n\n/**\n * JavaScript function call pattern.\n * Matches common JavaScript function calls that could execute code.\n * Captures the function name, opening paren, content, and closing paren.\n * Excludes \"script\" as a keyword to avoid false positives with HTML encoded entities\n */\nconst JAVASCRIPT_FUNCTION_PATTERN =\n  /\\b(alert|confirm|prompt|eval|exec|setTimeout|setInterval|Function|document\\.write|location\\.|window\\.|\\.innerHTML|\\.outerHTML)\\s*\\([^)]*\\)/gi;\n\n/**\n * SQL injection pattern - more specific to avoid false positives.\n *\n * Detects:\n * - Tautological conditions (1=1, 'a'='a')\n * - Comment sequences (--, slash-star, star-slash)\n * - UNION-based injections\n * - Stacked queries (semicolon)\n * - Boolean-based injection with quoted strings\n * - Time-based injection patterns\n *\n * Uses word boundaries and more specific patterns to reduce false positives\n * on legitimate text containing \"and\", \"or\", etc.\n */\nconst SQL_INJECTION_PATTERN =\n  // eslint-disable-next-line no-useless-escape\n  /(\\bOR\\b\\s+\\d+\\s*=\\s*\\d+)|(\\bAND\\b\\s+\\d+\\s*=\\s*\\d+)|('\\s*\\|\\|?\\s*')|('\\s*(OR|AND)\\s+'[^']+'\\s*=\\s*'[^']*')|('\\s*;)|(\\bor\\s+\\d+\\s*=\\s*\\d+\\b)|(\\band\\s+\\d+\\s*=\\s*\\d+\\b)|(\\bunion\\b\\s+\\bselect\\b)|(\\/\\*)|(\\*\\/)|(\\-\\-)|(;)|(waitfor\\s+delay)|(\\bsleep\\s*\\()/gi;\n\n/**\n * Command injection patterns.\n * Detects shell metacharacters and command chaining.\n * Note: $ is excluded here to avoid false positives with NoSQL operators like $ne\n */\nconst COMMAND_INJECTION_PATTERN = /[;&|`()<>]/g;\n\n/**\n * Path traversal patterns.\n * Detects attempts to access parent directories.\n */\nconst PATH_TRAVERSAL_PATTERN = /\\.\\.[/\\\\]/g;\n\n/**\n * LDAP injection patterns.\n * Detects attempts to inject LDAP queries.\n */\nconst LDAP_INJECTION_PATTERN = /(\\(|\\)|\\*|\\||&|\\||!=|>=|<=|=\\*|\\()/gi;\n\n/**\n * NoSQL injection patterns.\n * Detects attempts to inject NoSQL queries (MongoDB, etc.).\n */\nconst NOSQL_INJECTION_PATTERN = /(\\$where|\\$ne|\\$gt|\\$lt|\\$in|\\$or|\\$and|\\$regex|\\$expr)/gi;\n\n/**\n * XSS patterns beyond HTML tags.\n * Detects encoded and obfuscated XSS attempts.\n */\nconst XSS_PATTERN =\n  /(%3C|&lt;|&#60;|&#x3C;|%3E|&gt;|&#62;|&#x3E;|%22|&quot;|&#34;|&#x22;|%27|&apos;|&#39;|&#x27;)/gi;\n\n/**\n * Template injection patterns.\n * Detects attempts to inject template engine syntax.\n */\n\n// eslint-disable-next-line no-useless-escape\nconst TEMPLATE_INJECTION_PATTERN = /(\\{\\{|\\}\\}|\\$\\{|\\#{|@{{)/gi;\n\n/**\n * Log injection / log forging patterns.\n * Detects attempts to inject log entries with CRLF sequences.\n */\nconst LOG_INJECTION_PATTERN = /[\\r\\n]\\s*(ERROR|WARN|INFO|DEBUG|TRACE)/gi;\n\n/**\n * Sanitization options.\n */\nexport interface SanitizationOptions {\n  /** Strip HTML tags from string inputs */\n  stripHtml?: boolean;\n  /** Remove potential SQL injection patterns */\n  preventSqlInjection?: boolean;\n  /** Normalize whitespace (collapse multiple spaces) */\n  normalizeWhitespace?: boolean;\n  /** Remove command injection patterns */\n  preventCommandInjection?: boolean;\n  /** Remove path traversal patterns */\n  preventPathTraversal?: boolean;\n  /** Maximum string length (0 = no limit) */\n  maxLength?: number;\n  /** Remove LDAP injection patterns */\n  preventLdapInjection?: boolean;\n  /** Remove NoSQL injection patterns */\n  preventNosqlInjection?: boolean;\n  /** Remove XSS patterns including encoded variants */\n  preventXss?: boolean;\n  /** Remove template injection patterns */\n  preventTemplateInjection?: boolean;\n  /** Remove log injection patterns */\n  preventLogInjection?: boolean;\n  /** Preserve case (default: false - converts to lowercase) */\n  preserveCase?: boolean;\n  /** Preserve whitespace (default: false - normalizes) */\n  preserveWhitespace?: boolean;\n}\n\n/**\n * Default sanitization options.\n */\nconst DEFAULT_OPTIONS: SanitizationOptions = {\n  stripHtml: true,\n  preventSqlInjection: true,\n  preventCommandInjection: true,\n  preventPathTraversal: true,\n  normalizeWhitespace: true,\n  maxLength: 10000,\n  preventLdapInjection: true,\n  preventNosqlInjection: true,\n  preventXss: true,\n  preventTemplateInjection: true,\n  preventLogInjection: true,\n  preserveCase: false,\n  preserveWhitespace: false,\n};\n\n/**\n * Sanitization result with metadata.\n */\nexport interface SanitizationResult {\n  /** The sanitized value */\n  value: string;\n  /** Whether any sanitization was performed */\n  wasSanitized: boolean;\n  /** The type of sanitization performed (if any) */\n  sanitizationType?: string[];\n}\n\n/**\n * Sanitize a string value.\n *\n * Returns both the sanitized value and metadata about what was sanitized.\n * This is useful for logging and security monitoring.\n *\n * IMPORTANT: Pattern detection happens on the CURRENT value at each step,\n * which means patterns detected earlier may be removed by later sanitizations.\n * This is intentional to log all threats present in the original input.\n */\nexport function sanitizeString(\n  input: string,\n  options: SanitizationOptions = {}\n): SanitizationResult {\n  const mergedOptions = { ...DEFAULT_OPTIONS, ...options };\n  const sanitizationTypes: string[] = [];\n  let sanitized = input;\n\n  // Apply length limit first\n  if (mergedOptions.maxLength && mergedOptions.maxLength > 0) {\n    if (sanitized.length > mergedOptions.maxLength) {\n      sanitizationTypes.push(\"length_limit\");\n      sanitized = sanitized.slice(0, mergedOptions.maxLength);\n      logger.warn(\"Input exceeded maximum length, truncated\", {\n        originalLength: input.length,\n        maxLength: mergedOptions.maxLength,\n        preview: input.slice(0, 50),\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  // IMPORTANT: Detect ALL patterns first on the current sanitized value\n  // BEFORE any replacements that might remove the patterns.\n  // This ensures we log all threats present in the input.\n\n  // Detect SQL injection patterns\n  if (mergedOptions.preventSqlInjection && SQL_INJECTION_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {\n    sanitizationTypes.push(\"sql_injection\");\n    logger.warn(\"SQL injection pattern detected in input\", {\n      preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Detect command injection patterns\n  if (mergedOptions.preventCommandInjection && COMMAND_INJECTION_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {\n    sanitizationTypes.push(\"command_injection\");\n    logger.warn(\"Command injection pattern detected in input\", {\n      preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Detect path traversal patterns\n  if (mergedOptions.preventPathTraversal && PATH_TRAVERSAL_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {\n    sanitizationTypes.push(\"path_traversal\");\n    logger.warn(\"Path traversal pattern detected in input\", {\n      preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Detect LDAP injection patterns\n  if (mergedOptions.preventLdapInjection && LDAP_INJECTION_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {\n    sanitizationTypes.push(\"ldap_injection\");\n    logger.warn(\"LDAP injection pattern detected in input\", {\n      preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Detect NoSQL injection patterns\n  if (mergedOptions.preventNosqlInjection && NOSQL_INJECTION_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {\n    sanitizationTypes.push(\"nosql_injection\");\n    logger.warn(\"NoSQL injection pattern detected in input\", {\n      preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Detect XSS patterns (encoded and obfuscated)\n  if (mergedOptions.preventXss && XSS_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {\n    sanitizationTypes.push(\"xss_pattern\");\n    logger.warn(\"XSS pattern detected in input\", {\n      preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Detect template injection patterns\n  if (mergedOptions.preventTemplateInjection && TEMPLATE_INJECTION_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {\n    sanitizationTypes.push(\"template_injection\");\n    logger.warn(\"Template injection pattern detected in input\", {\n      preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Detect log injection patterns\n  if (mergedOptions.preventLogInjection && LOG_INJECTION_PATTERN.test(sanitized)) {\n    sanitizationTypes.push(\"log_injection\");\n    logger.warn(\"Log injection pattern detected in input\", {\n      preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Now apply the actual sanitization replacements\n\n  // Strip HTML tags\n  if (mergedOptions.stripHtml) {\n    // First, remove script and style tags WITH their content\n    const scriptStyleMatch = sanitized.match(SCRIPT_STYLE_PATTERN);\n    if (scriptStyleMatch) {\n      if (!sanitizationTypes.includes(\"html_tags\")) {\n        sanitizationTypes.push(\"html_tags\");\n      }\n      logger.warn(\"Script/style tags detected and removed from input\", {\n        pattern: scriptStyleMatch[0],\n        preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n      });\n    }\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(SCRIPT_STYLE_PATTERN, \" \");\n\n    // Then remove remaining HTML tags\n    const htmlMatch = sanitized.match(HTML_TAG_PATTERN);\n    if (htmlMatch) {\n      if (!sanitizationTypes.includes(\"html_tags\")) {\n        sanitizationTypes.push(\"html_tags\");\n      }\n      logger.warn(\"HTML tags detected and removed from input\", {\n        pattern: htmlMatch[0],\n        preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n      });\n    }\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(HTML_TAG_PATTERN, \"\");\n\n    // Remove event handlers\n    const eventHandlerMatch = sanitized.match(EVENT_HANDLER_PATTERN);\n    if (eventHandlerMatch) {\n      if (!sanitizationTypes.includes(\"html_tags\")) {\n        sanitizationTypes.push(\"event_handlers\");\n      }\n      logger.warn(\"Event handlers detected and removed from input\", {\n        pattern: eventHandlerMatch[0],\n        preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n      });\n    }\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(EVENT_HANDLER_PATTERN, \"\");\n\n    // Remove javascript: protocol\n    const jsProtocolMatch = sanitized.match(JAVASCRIPT_PROTOCOL_PATTERN);\n    if (jsProtocolMatch) {\n      if (!sanitizationTypes.includes(\"html_tags\")) {\n        sanitizationTypes.push(\"javascript_protocol\");\n      }\n      logger.warn(\"JavaScript protocol detected and removed from input\", {\n        preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n      });\n    }\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(JAVASCRIPT_PROTOCOL_PATTERN, \"\");\n\n    // Remove JavaScript function calls\n    const jsFunctionMatch = sanitized.match(JAVASCRIPT_FUNCTION_PATTERN);\n    if (jsFunctionMatch) {\n      if (!sanitizationTypes.includes(\"html_tags\")) {\n        sanitizationTypes.push(\"javascript_function\");\n      }\n      logger.warn(\"JavaScript function call detected and removed from input\", {\n        pattern: jsFunctionMatch[0],\n        preview: sanitized.slice(0, 50),\n      });\n    }\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(JAVASCRIPT_FUNCTION_PATTERN, \"\");\n  }\n\n  // Apply SQL injection sanitization\n  if (mergedOptions.preventSqlInjection) {\n    const beforeSqlSanitization = sanitized;\n    // Always remove dangerous SQL characters and keywords when SQL injection prevention is enabled\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/[;'\"]/g, \"\");\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/\\bor\\b/gi, \"\");\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/\\band\\b/gi, \"\");\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/\\bunion\\b/gi, \"\");\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/\\bselect\\b/gi, \"\");\n    // Check if sanitization actually changed anything\n    if (sanitized !== beforeSqlSanitization && !sanitizationTypes.includes(\"sql_injection\")) {\n      sanitizationTypes.push(\"sql_injection\");\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Apply command injection sanitization\n  if (mergedOptions.preventCommandInjection) {\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(COMMAND_INJECTION_PATTERN, \"\");\n  }\n\n  // Apply path traversal sanitization\n  if (mergedOptions.preventPathTraversal) {\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(PATH_TRAVERSAL_PATTERN, \"\");\n  }\n\n  // Apply LDAP injection sanitization\n  if (mergedOptions.preventLdapInjection) {\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/[()|&*!=]/g, \"\");\n  }\n\n  // Apply NoSQL injection sanitization\n  if (mergedOptions.preventNosqlInjection) {\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/\\$[a-zA-Z]+/g, \"\");\n  }\n\n  // Apply XSS pattern sanitization\n  if (mergedOptions.preventXss) {\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/%3C|%3E|%22|%27|%3D|%3B/gi, \"\");\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/&lt;|&gt;|&quot;|&apos;/gi, \"\");\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/&#\\d+;/gi, \"\");\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/&#x[0-9a-fA-F]+;/gi, \"\");\n  }\n\n  // Apply template injection sanitization\n  if (mergedOptions.preventTemplateInjection) {\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/\\{\\{|\\}\\}|\\$\\{|#\\{/g, \"\");\n  }\n\n  // Apply log injection sanitization\n  if (mergedOptions.preventLogInjection) {\n    // Remove log level prefixes after newlines\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/[\\r\\n]+\\s*(ERROR|WARN|INFO|DEBUG|TRACE)/gi, \" \");\n  }\n\n  // Normalize whitespace\n  if (mergedOptions.normalizeWhitespace) {\n    const normalized = sanitized.replace(/\\s+/g, \" \").trim();\n    if (normalized !== sanitized) {\n      sanitizationTypes.push(\"whitespace_normalization\");\n    }\n    sanitized = normalized;\n  }\n\n  return {\n    value: sanitized,\n    wasSanitized: sanitizationTypes.length > 0,\n    sanitizationType: sanitizationTypes.length > 0 ? sanitizationTypes : undefined,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Sanitize a string value (simplified version, returns just the string).\n * Use this when you don't need the metadata.\n */\nexport function sanitizeStringSimple(input: string, options: SanitizationOptions = {}): string {\n  return sanitizeString(input, options).value;\n}\n\n/**\n * Sanitize a string value for use as an object key.\n *\n * Preserves content between tags while removing the tags themselves.\n * This is more appropriate for keys where we want to keep the readable text.\n */\nfunction sanitizeKey(key: string, options: SanitizationOptions = {}): string {\n  let sanitized = key;\n\n  // Strip only the tags, not the content\n  // Default to stripHtml: true for keys to prevent tag-based injection\n  const shouldStripHtml = options.stripHtml !== false;\n\n  if (shouldStripHtml) {\n    // First remove script/style tags with content (for security)\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(SCRIPT_STYLE_PATTERN, \" \");\n    // Then remove remaining HTML tags (preserving content)\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(HTML_TAG_PATTERN, \"\");\n  }\n\n  // Apply other sanitizations\n  if (options.preventCommandInjection !== false) {\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(COMMAND_INJECTION_PATTERN, \"\");\n  }\n  if (options.preventPathTraversal !== false) {\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(PATH_TRAVERSAL_PATTERN, \"\");\n  }\n  if (options.preventSqlInjection !== false) {\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/[;'\"]/g, \"\");\n  }\n\n  // Normalize whitespace\n  if (options.normalizeWhitespace !== false) {\n    sanitized = sanitized.replace(/\\s+/g, \" \").trim();\n  }\n\n  return sanitized;\n}\n\n/**\n * Recursively sanitize string values in an object.\n *\n * Handles:\n * - Primitive strings\n * - Nested objects\n * - Arrays\n * - null and undefined values\n */\nexport function sanitizeObject(obj: unknown, options: SanitizationOptions = {}): unknown {\n  if (typeof obj === \"string\") {\n    return sanitizeStringSimple(obj, options);\n  }\n\n  if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n    return obj.map((item) => sanitizeObject(item, options));\n  }\n\n  if (obj !== null && typeof obj === \"object\") {\n    const sanitized: Record<string, unknown> = {};\n    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(obj)) {\n      // Also sanitize object keys to prevent key-based injection\n      // Use key sanitization which preserves content between tags\n      const sanitizedKey = sanitizeKey(key, options);\n      const sanitizedValue = sanitizeObject(value, options);\n      sanitized[sanitizedKey] = sanitizedValue;\n    }\n    return sanitized;\n  }\n\n  return obj;\n}\n\n/**\n * Sanitize query parameters.\n *\n * Handles both single values and arrays.\n */\nexport function sanitizeParams(\n  params: Record<string, string | string[] | undefined>,\n  options: SanitizationOptions = {}\n): Record<string, string> {\n  const sanitized: Record<string, string> = {};\n\n  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(params)) {\n    // Sanitize the key as well\n    const sanitizedKey = sanitizeStringSimple(key, {\n      ...options,\n      normalizeWhitespace: false,\n    });\n\n    if (typeof value === \"string\") {\n      sanitized[sanitizedKey] = sanitizeStringSimple(value, options);\n    } else if (Array.isArray(value)) {\n      // For arrays, take the first value and sanitize it\n      sanitized[sanitizedKey] = sanitizeStringSimple(value[0] || \"\", options);\n    } else {\n      // undefined or null - use empty string\n      sanitized[sanitizedKey] = \"\";\n    }\n  }\n\n  return sanitized;\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate and sanitize an API key ID format.\n *\n * API key IDs should be alphanumeric with underscores, 3-50 chars.\n */\nexport function validateApiKeyFormat(keyId: string): boolean {\n  return /^[A-Za-z0-9_]{3,50}$/.test(keyId);\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate and sanitize a session token format.\n *\n * Session tokens should be UUID v4 format.\n */\nexport function validateSessionTokenFormat(token: string): boolean {\n  return /^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i.test(token);\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate and sanitize an HMAC signature format.\n *\n * Signatures should be in format: hmac_sha256:<64_hex_chars>\n */\nexport function validateSignatureFormat(signature: string): boolean {\n  return /^hmac_sha256:[a-f0-9]{64}$/i.test(signature);\n}\n\n/**\n * Encode output for HTML context to prevent XSS.\n * Escapes <, >, &, \", and ' characters.\n */\nexport function encodeHtmlOutput(unsafe: string): string {\n  return unsafe\n    .replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\")\n    .replace(/</g, \"&lt;\")\n    .replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\")\n    .replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\")\n    .replace(/'/g, \"&#39;\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Encode output for JavaScript string context to prevent XSS.\n * Escapes backslashes, quotes, and other special characters.\n */\nexport function encodeJsOutput(unsafe: string): string {\n  return unsafe\n    .replace(/\\\\/g, \"\\\\\\\\\")\n    .replace(/'/g, \"\\\\'\")\n    .replace(/\"/g, '\\\\\"')\n    .replace(/\\n/g, \"\\\\n\")\n    .replace(/\\r/g, \"\\\\r\")\n    .replace(/\\t/g, \"\\\\t\")\n    .replace(/\\f/g, \"\\\\f\")\n    .replace(/\\v/g, \"\\\\v\")\n    .replace(/\\0/g, \"\\\\0\");\n}\n\n/**\n * Encode output for URL parameter context.\n */\nexport function encodeUrlOutput(unsafe: string): string {\n  return encodeURIComponent(unsafe);\n}\n\n/**\n * Encode output for CSS context.\n * Escapes special characters that could break out of CSS strings.\n */\nexport function encodeCssOutput(unsafe: string): string {\n  return
