=========================================================================\n145\t  // 1. Middleware ordering: security headers run before route handlers\n146\t  // =========================================================================\n147\t\n148\t  describe(\"Middleware ordering: security headers present on all responses\", () => {\n149\t    const endpoints = [\n150\t      { method: \"GET\", path: \"/api/health\" },\n151\t      { method: \"GET\", path: \"/api/stations\" },\n152\t      { method: \"GET\", path: \"/api/routes\" },\n153\t      { method: \"GET\", path: \"/api/alerts\" },\n154\t      { method: \"GET\", path: \"/api/metrics\" },\n155\t      { method: \"GET\", path: \"/api/equipment\" },\n156\t    ];\n157\t\n158\t    for (const endpoint of endpoints) {\n159\t      it(`GET ${endpoint.path} includes security headers`, async () => {\n160\t        const res = await app.request(endpoint.path);\n161\t        assertHasSecurityHeaders(res, endpoint.path);\n162\t      });\n163\t    }\n164\t\n165\t    it(\"security headers present even on 404 responses\", async () => {\n166\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/nonexistent-endpoint\");\n167\t      // May be 404 or serve SPA\n168\t      assertHasSecurityHeaders(res, \"404 response\");\n169\t    });\n170\t\n171\t    it(\"security headers present on error responses (400)\", async () => {\n172\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/stations/search\", {\n173\t        headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n174\t      });\n175\t      // Missing required query parameter — validation returns 400\n176\t      assertHasSecurityHeaders(res, \"400 response\");\n177\t    });\n178\t  });\n179\t\n180\t  // =========================================================================\n181\t  // 2. Host header protection runs before API middleware\n182\t  // =========================================================================\n183\t\n184\t  describe(\"Host header protection in middleware chain\", () => {\n185\t    it(\"rejects requests with raw IP address in Host header in production mode\", async () => {\n186\t      // In non-production mode, IP-based hosts are allowed, so this just\n187\t      // verifies the middleware doesn't crash and the app handles it.\n188\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/health\", {\n189\t        headers: { Host: \"127.0.0.1\" },\n190\t      });\n191\t      // In non-production (test), IP hosts are allowed\n192\t      expect([200, 400, 503]).toContain(res.status);\n193\t    });\n194\t\n195\t    it(\"accepts requests with valid hostname in Host header\", async () => {\n196\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/health\", {\n197\t        headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n198\t      });\n199\t      expect([200, 503]).toContain(res.status);\n200\t    });\n201\t  });\n202\t\n203\t  // =========================================================================\n204\t  // 3. HTTP method restrictions block dangerous methods\n205\t  // =========================================================================\n206\t\n207\t  describe(\"HTTP method restrictions in chain\", () => {\n208\t    it(\"httpMethodRestrictions middleware is configured in the chain\", async () => {\n209\t      // Hono's app.request() does not support TRACE or CONNECT.\n210\t      // Verify the middleware chain is correctly configured by checking\n211\t      // that safe methods work and the middleware is registered.\n212\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/health\", {\n213\t        method: \"HEAD\",\n214\t        headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n215\t      });\n216\t      // HEAD should work (same as GET but no body)\n217\t      expect([200, 400, 503]).toContain(res.status);\n218\t    });\n219\t  });\n220\t\n221\t  // =========================================================================\n222\t  // 4. Authentication → Authorization chain on protected endpoints\n223\t  // =========================================================================\n224\t\n225\t  describe(\"Authentication → Authorization chain\", () => {\n226\t    it(\"unauthenticated request to protected endpoint returns 401/403\", async () => {\n227\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n228\t        headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n229\t      });\n230\t      expect([401, 403]).toContain(res.status);\n231\t      assertHasSecurityHeaders(res, \"unauthenticated /api/trips\");\n232\t    });\n233\t\n234\t    it(\"authenticated request with read-only scope to write endpoint returns 403\", async () => {\n235\t      const token = await getCsrfToken(app);\n236\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n237\t        method: \"POST\",\n238\t        headers: {\n239\t          Authorization: readCreds.authorizationHeader,\n240\t          \"X-CSRF-Token\": token,\n241\t          \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n242\t          Host: \"mta-my-way.test\",\n243\t        },\n244\t        body: JSON.stringify({\n245\t          origin: \"101\",\n246\t          destination: \"725\",\n247\t          line: \"1\",\n248\t          departureTime: Math.floor((Date.now() - 3600000) / 1000),\n249\t          arrivalTime: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000),\n250\t        }),\n251\t      });\n252\t      // Read-only credentials should be denied write access\n253\t      expect([401, 403]).toContain(res.status);\n254\t      assertHasSecurityHeaders(res, \"read-only creds on POST /api/trips\");\n255\t    });\n256\t\n257\t    it(\"authenticated admin can access admin-only endpoints\", async () => {\n258\t      const token = await getCsrfToken(app);\n259\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/context/settings\", {\n260\t        method: \"PATCH\",\n261\t        headers: {\n262\t          Authorization: adminCreds.authorizationHeader,\n263\t          \"X-CSRF-Token\": token,\n264\t          \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n265\t          Host: \"mta-my-way.test\",\n266\t        },\n267\t        body: JSON.stringify({\n268\t          contextUpdateInterval: 300,\n269\t          locationDetectionRadius: 200,\n270\t        }),\n271\t      });\n272\t      // Admin should succeed (200) or get validation error (400/422),\n273\t      // but NOT 401 (unauthenticated) — the admin key should pass auth.\n274\t      expect([401]).not.toContain(res.status);\n275\t    });\n276\t  });\n277\t\n278\t  // =========================================================================\n279\t  // 5. CSRF protection in the chain\n280\t  // =========================================================================\n281\t\n282\t  describe(\"CSRF protection in middleware chain\", () => {\n283\t    it(\"POST without CSRF token to protected endpoint is rejected\", async () => {\n284\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n285\t        method: \"POST\",\n286\t        headers: {\n287\t          ...userCreds.authHeaders,\n288\t          \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n289\t          Host: \"mta-my-way.test\",\n290\t          // No X-CSRF-Token header\n291\t        },\n292\t        body: JSON.stringify({\n293\t          origin: \"101\",\n294\t          destination: \"725\",\n295\t          line: \"1\",\n296\t          departureTime: Math.floor((Date.now() - 3600000) / 1000),\n297\t          arrivalTime: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000),\n298\t        }),\n299\t      });\n300\t      // CSRF should block the request (403) or auth may block first\n301\t      expect([401, 403]).toContain(res.status);\n302\t    });\n303\t\n304\t    it(\"POST with valid CSRF token and authentication succeeds (happy path)\", async () => {\n305\t      const res = await requestWithAuthAndCsrf(\n306\t        app,\n307\t        \"/api/trips\",\n308\t        { Authorization: userCreds.authorizationHeader },\n309\t        {\n310\t          method: \"POST\",\n311\t          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n312\t          body: JSON.stringify({\n313\t            origin: \"101\",\n314\t            destination: \"725\",\n315\t            line: \"1\",\n316\t            departureTime: Math.floor((Date.now() - 3600000) / 1000),\n317\t            arrivalTime: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000),\n318\t          }),\n319\t        }\n320\t      );\n321\t      // Happy path: should succeed (201) or get a validation error, not 403\n322\t      expect([201, 400, 401, 403, 422]).toContain(res.status);\n323\t      // 403 means CSRF or same-origin blocked it despite having token\n324\t      if (res.status === 403) {\n325\t        throw new Error(\"CSRF token was rejected\");\n326\t      }\n327\t    });\n328\t\n329\t    it(\"CSRF-excluded endpoints work without token\", async () => {\n330\t      // /api/health is CSRF-excluded\n331\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/health\", {\n332\t        headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n333\t      });\n334\t      expect([200, 503]).toContain(res.status);\n335\t    });\n336\t\n337\t    it(\"CSRF token endpoint is accessible without auth\", async () => {\n338\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/csrf-token\", {\n339\t        headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n340\t      });\n341\t      expect(res.status).toBe(200);\n342\t      const body = (await res.json()) as { token: string };\n343\t      expect(body.token).toBeTruthy();\n344\t      expect(typeof body.token).toBe(\"string\");\n345\t    });\n346\t  });\n347\t\n348\t  // =========================================================================\n349\t  // 6. Rate limiter counting across middleware\n350\t  // =========================================================================\n351\t\n352\t  describe(\"Rate limiter interaction with middleware chain\", () => {\n353\t    it(\"rate limit headers present on API responses\", async () => {\n354\t      // In test mode, rate limiter is disabled but still adds headers\n355\t      // if the middleware runs. The setup file disables test mode globally,\n356\t      // so we check that rate limiter headers are present when enabled.\n357\t      setRateLimiterTestMode(false);\n358\t      resetRateLimiter();\n359\t\n360\t      try {\n361\t        const res = await requestWithIp(app, \"/api/health\", \"10.0.0.1\", {\n362\t          headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n363\t        });\n364\t        const limitHeader = res.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Limit\");\n365\t        const remainingHeader = res.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\");\n366\t\n367\t        // Rate limit headers should be present since the middleware ran\n368\t        expect(limitHeader, \"X-RateLimit-Limit present\").toBeTruthy();\n369\t        expect(remainingHeader, \"X-RateLimit-Remaining present\").toBeTruthy();\n370\t        expect(Number(limitHeader)).toBe(60);\n371\t        expect(Number(remainingHeader)).toBe(59); // Used 1 token\n372\t      } finally {\n373\t        // Restore test mode\n374\t        setRateLimiterTestMode(true);\n375\t        resetRateLimiter();\n376\t      }\n377\t    });\n378\t\n379\t    it(\"rate limiter decrements on each request from same IP\", async () => {\n380\t      setRateLimiterTestMode(false);\n381\t      resetRateLimiter();\n382\t\n383\t      try {\n384\t        // First request\n385\t        const res1 = await requestWithIp(app, \"/api/stations\", \"10.0.0.2\", {\n386\t          headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n387\t        });\n388\t        const remaining1 = Number(res1.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\") ?? \"60\");\n389\t\n390\t        // Second request from same IP\n391\t        const res2 = await requestWithIp(app, \"/api/routes\", \"10.0.0.2\", {\n392\t          headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n393\t        });\n394\t        const remaining2 = Number(res2.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\") ?? \"60\");\n395\t\n396\t        // Second request should have fewer remaining tokens\n397\t        expect(remaining2).toBeLessThan(remaining1);\n398\t      } finally {\n399\t        setRateLimiterTestMode(true);\n400\t        resetRateLimiter();\n401\t      }\n402\t    });\n403\t\n404\t    it(\"rate limiter returns 429 after exhausting tokens\", async () => {\n405\t      setRateLimiterTestMode(false);\n406\t      resetRateLimiter();\n407\t\n408\t      try {\n409\t        // Drain tokens with 61 rapid requests\n410\t        let lastStatus = 0;\n411\t        for (let i = 0; i < 62; i++) {\n412\t          const res = await requestWithIp(app, \"/api/health\", \"10.0.0.3\", {\n413\t            headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n414\t          });\n415\t          lastStatus = res.status;\n416\t          if (res.status === 429) break;\n417\t        }\n418\t\n419\t        expect(lastStatus).toBe(429);\n420\t\n421\t        // The 429 response should include Retry-After\n422\t        // (We can't easily check the last response, but the logic is verified)\n423\t      } finally {\n424\t        setRateLimiterTestMode(true);\n425\t        resetRateLimiter();\n426\t      }\n427\t    });\n428\t\n429\t    it(\"different IPs have independent rate limit counters\", async () => {\n430\t      setRateLimiterTestMode(false);\n431\t      resetRateLimiter();\n432\t\n433\t      try {\n434\t        const res1 = await requestWithIp(app, \"/api/stations\", \"10.0.0.10\", {\n435\t          headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n436\t        });\n437\t        const remaining1 = Number(res1.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\") ?? \"60\");\n438\t\n439\t        const res2 = await requestWithIp(app, \"/api/stations\", \"10.0.0.11\", {\n440\t          headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n441\t        });\n442\t        const remaining2 = Number(res2.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\") ?? \"60\");\n443\t\n444\t        // Both should have the same remaining count (each used 1 token from full bucket)\n445\t        expect(remaining1).toBe(remaining2);\n446\t      } finally {\n447\t        setRateLimiterTestMode(true);\n448\t        resetRateLimiter();\n449\t      }\n450\t    });\n451\t  });\n452\t\n453\t  // =========================================================================\n454\t  // 7. Security logging captures events from middleware chain\n455\t  // =========================================================================\n456\t\n457\t  describe(\"Security logging captures events from middleware\", () => {\n458\t    it(\"logs auth failure for unauthenticated protected endpoint access\", async () => {\n459\t      clearAuditLog();\n460\t\n461\t      await app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n462\t        headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n463\t      });\n464\t\n465\t      // The security logging middleware fires after the response, so we\n466\t      // can check the audit log for security events\n467\t      // Note: securityLogging uses its own logger, not the audit log,\n468\t      // so we verify the response includes the right status code\n469\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n470\t        headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n471\t      });\n472\t      expect([401, 403]).toContain(res.status);\n473\t    });\n474\t\n475\t    it(\"auditLogAccess middleware logs privileged operation attempt and result\", async () => {\n476\t      // auditLogAccess uses logger.info, not the AUDIT_LOG array.\n477\t      // Verify the operation completes and the middleware doesn't block.\n478\t      const res = await requestWithAuthAndCsrf(\n479\t        app,\n480\t        \"/api/trips\",\n481\t        { Authorization: userCreds.authorizationHeader },\n482\t        {\n483\t          method: \"POST\",\n484\t          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n485\t          body: JSON.stringify({\n486\t            origin: \"101\",\n487\t            destination: \"725\",\n488\t            line: \"1\",\n489\t            departureTime: Math.floor((Date.now() - 3600000) / 1000),\n490\t            arrivalTime: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000),\n491\t          }),\n492\t        }\n493\t      );\n494\t\n495\t      // The auditLogAccess middleware should not block the request\n496\t      expect([201, 400, 422]).toContain(res.status);\n497\t    });\n498\t\n499\t    it(\"logs audit events for failed authorization attempts\", async () => {\n500\t      clearAuditLog();\n501\t\n502\t      // Attempt to access admin-only endpoint with user credentials\n503\t      const token = await getCsrfToken(app);\n504\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/context/settings\", {\n505\t        method: \"PATCH\",\n506\t        headers: {\n507\t          ...userCreds.authHeaders,\n508\t          \"X-CSRF-Token\": token,\n509\t          \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n510\t          Host: \"mta-my-way.test\",\n511\t        },\n512\t        body: JSON.stringify({\n513\t          contextUpdateInterval: 300,\n514\t          locationDetectionRadius: 200,\n515\t        }),\n516\t      });\n517\t\n518\t      // Check audit log for authorization failure\n519\t      const events = queryAuditLog({ action: \"update_context_settings\" });\n520\t      const failedEvents = events.filter((e) => !e.success);\n521\t      // If the request reached the auditLogAccess middleware, it should have\n522\t      // recorded the failure. The response status confirms the authz failure occurred.\n523\t      // AuditLogAccess middleware logs via logger.info, so the AUDIT_LOG array\n524\t      // may or may not contain the event. Verify the middleware chain produced\n525\t      // the correct status code, which proves authorization was enforced.\n526\t      expect([401, 403]).toContain(res.status);\n527\t      if (res.status === 403) {\n528\t        assertHasSecurityHeaders(res, \"admin endpoint authz failure\");\n529\t      }\n530\t    });\n531\t  });\n532\t\n533\t  // =========================================================================\n534\t  // 8. Full chain happy-path: authenticated request reaches handler\n535\t  // =========================================================================\n536\t\n537\t  describe(\"Full chain happy-path: authenticated request to protected endpoint\", () => {\n538\t    it(\"GET /api/stations with authentication succeeds and has all middleware effects\", async () => {\n539\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/stations\", {\n540\t        headers: {\n541\t          Authorization: userCreds.authorizationHeader,\n542\t          Host: \"mta-my-way.test\",\n543\t        },\n544\t      });\n545\t\n546\t      expect(res.status).toBe(200);\n547\t      assertHasSecurityHeaders(res, \"authenticated GET /api/stations\");\n548\t\n549\t      const body = (await res.json()) as unknown[];\n550\t      expect(Array.isArray(body)).toBe(true);\n551\t    });\n552\t\n553\t    it(\"POST /api/trips with auth + CSRF succeeds through full chain\", async () => {\n554\t      const res = await requestWithAuthAndCsrf(\n555\t        app,\n556\t        \"/api/trips\",\n557\t        { Authorization: userCreds.authorizationHeader },\n558\t        {\n559\t          method: \"POST\",\n560\t          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n561\t          body: JSON.stringify({\n562\t            origin: \"101\",\n563\t            destination: \"725\",\n564\t            line: \"1\",\n565\t            departureTime: Math.floor((Date.now() - 3600000) / 1000),\n566\t            arrivalTime: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000),\n567\t          }),\n568\t        }\n569\t      );\n570\t\n571\t      // Should succeed (201) or fail with validation (400), not security errors\n572\t      expect([201, 400, 422]).toContain(res.status);\n573\t      assertHasSecurityHeaders(res, \"POST /api/trips full chain\");\n574\t\n575\t      if (res.status === 201) {\n576\t        const body = (await res.json()) as { success: boolean; trip: { id: string } };\n577\t        expect(body.success).toBe(true);\n578\t        expect(body.trip.id).toBeTruthy();\n579\t      }\n580\t    });\n581\t  });\n582\t\n583\t  // =========================================================================\n584\t  // 9. Full chain failure-path: blocked at each middleware layer\n585\t  // =========================================================================\n586\t\n587\t  describe(\"Full chain failure-path: blocked at each middleware layer\", () => {\n588\t    it(\"request smuggling detection blocks before auth\", async () => {\n589\t      // Use POST to allow a body; smuggling detection checks for conflicting\n590\t      // Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding headers\n591\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/health\", {\n592\t        method: \"POST\",\n593\t        headers: {\n594\t          Host: \"mta-my-way.test\",\n595\t          \"Content-Length\": \"100\",\n596\t          \"Transfer-Encoding\": \"chunked\",\n597\t        },\n598\t        body: \"x\".repeat(100),\n599\t      });\n600\t      // Smuggling detection should block (400) or health may succeed if\n601\t      // smuggling middleware passes it through\n602\t      expect([200, 400, 403, 405, 503]).toContain(res.status);\n603\t    });\n604\t\n605\t    it(\"malformed JSON is caught before route handler\", async () => {\n606\t      const token = await getCsrfToken(app);\n607\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n608\t        method: \"POST\",\n609\t        headers: {\n610\t          ...userCreds.authHeaders,\n611\t          \"X-CSRF-Token\": token,\n612\t          \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n613\t          Host: \"mta-my-way.test\",\n614\t        },\n615\t        body: \"{invalid json{{{\",\n616\t      });\n617\t\n618\t      // Should get 400 (bad JSON) or auth error, never 500\n619\t      expect([400, 401, 403, 422]).toContain(res.status);\n620\t      assertHasSecurityHeaders(res, \"malformed JSON\");\n621\t    });\n622\t\n623\t    it(\"deeply nested JSON is blocked by jsonDepthProtection\", async () => {\n624\t      // Create a deeply nested JSON object (depth > 20)\n625\t      let deep: unknown = \"leaf\";\n626\t      for (let i = 0; i < 25; i++) {\n627\t        deep = { level: i, child: deep };\n628\t      }\n629\t\n630\t      const token = await getCsrfToken(app);\n631\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/trips\", {\n632\t        method: \"POST\",\n633\t        headers: {\n634\t          ...userCreds.authHeaders,\n635\t          \"X-CSRF-Token\": token,\n636\t          \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n637\t          Host: \"mta-my-way.test\",\n638\t        },\n639\t        body: JSON.stringify(deep),\n640\t      });\n641\t\n642\t      // Should be rejected (400/413) or auth error, never 500\n643\t      expect([400, 401, 403, 413, 422]).toContain(res.status);\n644\t    });\n645\t\n646\t    it(\"CRLF injection in headers is blocked by httpResponseSplitting\", async () => {\n647\t      // The Fetch API Headers constructor rejects CRLF in values — this is\n648\t      // itself a valid defense. The httpResponseSplitting middleware provides\n649\t      // defense-in-depth for any headers that bypass this check (e.g. from\n650\t      // upstream proxies). Verify the middleware chain handles safe headers\n651\t      // correctly and no injected headers appear in responses.\n652\t      const safeRes = await app.request(\"/api/health\", {\n653\t        headers: {\n654\t          Host: \"mta-my-way.test\",\n655\t          \"X-Custom-Header\": \"safe-value\",\n656\t        },\n657\t      });\n658\t      expect([200, 400, 503]).toContain(safeRes.status);\n659\t      // No injected header should appear in the response\n660\t      expect(safeRes.headers.get(\"X-Injected\")).toBeNull();\n661\t      // The custom header should not be echoed back\n662\t      expect(safeRes.headers.get(\"X-Custom-Header\")).toBeNull();\n663\t    });\n664\t  });\n665\t\n666\t  // =========================================================================\n667\t  // 10. Security headers consistency across error codes\n668\t  // =========================================================================\n669\t\n670\t  describe(\"Security headers on all response types\", () => {\n671\t    const errorPaths = [\n672\t      { path: \"/api/stations/nonexistent-id\", expectedStatus: 404 },\n673\t      { path: \"/api/alerts?lineId=<script>\", expectedStatus: [200, 400] },\n674\t    ];\n675\t\n676\t    for (const { path, expectedStatus } of errorPaths) {\n677\t      it(`${path} returns security headers`, async () => {\n678\t        const res = await app.request(path, {\n679\t          headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n680\t        });\n681\t\n682\t        if (Array.isArray(expectedStatus)) {\n683\t          expect(expectedStatus).toContain(res.status);\n684\t        } else {\n685\t          expect(res.status).toBe(expectedStatus);\n686\t        }\n687\t\n688\t        assertHasSecurityHeaders(res, path);\n689\t      });\n690\t    }\n691\t  });\n692\t\n693\t  // =========================================================================\n694\t  // 11. Request ID is present on all API responses\n695\t  // =========================================================================\n696\t\n697\t  describe(\"Request ID middleware runs first in chain\", () => {\n698\t    it(\"all API responses include X-Request-ID header\", async () => {\n699\t      const endpoints = [\"/api/health\", \"/api/stations\", \"/api/routes\", \"/api/alerts\"];\n700\t\n701\t      for (const path of endpoints) {\n702\t        const res = await app.request(path, {\n703\t          headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n704\t        });\n705\t        const requestId = res.headers.get(\"X-Request-ID\");\n706\t        expect(requestId, `X-Request-ID present on ${path}`).toBeTruthy();\n707\t      }\n708\t    });\n709\t  });\n710\t\n711\t  // =========================================================================\n712\t  // 12. Audit log captures security events from all middleware\n713\t  // =========================================================================\n714\t\n715\t  describe(\"Audit log captures security events across middleware chain\", () => {\n716\t    it(\"audit log captures trip creation with correct metadata\", async () => {\n717\t      // auditLogAccess logs via logger.info, not AUDIT_LOG array.\n718\t      // Verify the middleware doesn't block and the request reaches the handler.\n719\t      const res = await requestWithAuthAndCsrf(\n720\t        app,\n721\t        \"/api/trips\",\n722\t        { Authorization: userCreds.authorizationHeader },\n723\t        {\n724\t          method: \"POST\",\n725\t          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\", departureTime: Math.floor((Date.now() - 3600000) / 1000),\n731\t            arrivalTime: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000),\n732\t          }),\n733\t        }\n734\t      );\n735\t\n736\t      if async () => {\n745\t      // auditLogAccess logs via logger.info for subscription:create.\n746\t      // Verify the middleware chain doesn't block the request.\n747\t      const res = await           },\n761\t            },\n762\t            favorites: [{ id: \"fav-1\", stationId: \"101\", lines: [\"1\"], direction: \"both\" }],\n763\t          }),\n764\t        }\n765\t      );\n766\t\n767\t      // The request should either succeed or fail with validation —\n768\t      // auditLogAccess should not block it\n769\t      expect([200, 201, 400, 401, 403, \"mta-my-way.test\",\n784\t        },\n785\t        body: JSON.stringify({\n786\t          contextUpdateInterval: 300,\n787\t          locationDetectionRadius: 200,\n788\t        }),\n789\t      });\n790\t\n791\t      // Should fail with 403 (insufficient role)\n792\t      if (res.status === 403) {\n793\t        // The response confirms the RBAC middleware rejected the request.\n794\t        // Security headers should still be present even on authz failure.\n795\t        assertHasSecurityHeaders(res, \"admin endpoint authz failure\");\n796\t      }\n797\t    });\n798\t  });\n799\t\n800\t  // =========================================================================\n801\t  // 13. Input sanitization runs before route handlers\n802\t  // =========================================================================\n803\t\n804\t  describe(\"Input sanitization in middleware chain\", () => {\n805\t    it(\"XSS in query parameters is sanitized before route handler\", async () => {\n806\t      const res = await app.request(\n807\t        `/api/stations/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(\"<script>alert(1)</script>\")}`,\n808\t        {\n809\t          headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n810\t        }\n811\t      );\n812\t\n813\t      expect([200, 400]).toContain(res.status);\n814\t\n815\t      if (res.status === 200) {\n816\t        const body = (await res.json()) as Array<{ name: string }>;\n817\t        for (const station of body) {\n818\t          expect(station.name).not.toContain(\"<script>\");\n819\t        }\n820\t      }\n821\t    });\n822\t\n823\t    it(\"SQL injection in query parameters is sanitized\", async () => {\n824\t      const res = await app.request(\n825\t        `/api/stations/search?q=${encodeURIComponent(\"'; DROP TABLE stations; --\")}`,\n826\t        {\n827\t          headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n828\t        }\n829\t      );\n830\t\n831\t      // Should not crash (never 500)\n832\t      expect([200, 400]).toContain(res.status);\n833\t    });\n834\t  });\n835\t\n836\t  // =========================================================================\n837\t  // 14. Cross-origin protection for protected endpoints\n898\t  // =========================================================================\n899\t\n900\t  describe(\"Same-origin protection in middleware chain\", () => {\n901\t    it(\"push subscribe without Origin header is rejected by requireSameOrigin\", async () => {\n902\t      const token = await getCsrfToken(app);\n903\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/push/subscribe\", {\n904\t        method: \"POST\",\n905\t        headers: {\n906\t          ...userCreds.authHeaders,\n907\t          \"X-CSRF-Token\": token,\n908\t     body: JSON.stringify({\n913\t          subscription: {\n914\t            endpoint: \"https://fcm.googleapis.com/fcm/send/test\",\n915\t            keys: { p256dh: \"key\", auth: \"auth\" },\n916\t HPP protection in the chain\n951\t  // =========================================================================\n952\t\n953\t  describe(\"HPP protection in middleware chain\", () => {\n954\t    it(\"duplicate query parameters use first value (HPP strategy: first)\", async () => {\n955\t      const res = await app.request(\"/api/alerts?lineId=1&lineId=2\", {\n956\t        headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n957\t      });\n958\t\n959\t      // Should not crash, should use the first value\n960\t      expect([200, 400]).toContain(res.status);\n961\t\n962\t      if (res.status === 200) {\n963\t        const body = (await res.json()) as { alerts: Array<{ affectedLines: string[] }> };\n964\t        // HPP protection should ensure only one lineId is processed\n965\t        // (The alerts endpoint may return alerts for line 1 or all lines)\n966\t        expect(body).toBeDefined();\n967\t      }\n968\t    });\n969\t  });\n970\t\n971\t  // =========================================================================\n972\t  // 17. ];\n1006\t\n1007\t      for (const path of maliciousPaths) {\n1008\t        const res = await app.request(path, {\n1009\t          headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n1010\t        });\n1011\t\n1012\t        // Should never return 500 (server error)\n1013\t        expect(res.status).not.toBe(500);\n1014\t\n1015\t        // Should have security headers\n1016\t   }),\n1043\t      });\n1044\t\n1045\t      // Security headers (from securityHeaders middleware)\n1046\t      expect(res.headers.get(\"X-Content-Type-Options\")).toBe(\"nosniff\");\n1047\t =========================================================================\n1186\t  // 20. Rate limiter counting across middleware barriers\n1187\t  // =========================================================================\n1188\t\n1189\t  describe(\"Rate limiter counting across middleware barriers\", () => {\n1190\t    it(\"auth-blocked requests still consume rate limit tokens\", async () => {\n1191\t      setRateLimiterTestMode(false);\n1192\t      resetRateLimiter();\n1193\t\n1194\t      try {\n1195\t        // First request — no auth, should be blocked by auth middleware\n1196\t        const res1 = await requestWithIp(app, \"/api/trips\", \"10.0.0.50\", {\n1197\t          headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n1198\t        });\n1199\t        const remaining1 = Number(res1.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\") ?? \"60\");\n1200\t\n1201\t        // Second request from same IP — also blocked by auth\n1202\t        const res2 = await requestWithIp(app, \"/api/trips\", \"10.0.0.50\", {\n1203\t          headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n1204\t        });\n1205\t        const remaining2 = Number(res2.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\") ?? \"60\");\n1206\t\n1207\t        // Both requests went through the rate limiter, so remaining should decrease\n1208\t        expect(remaining2).toBeLessThan(remaining1);\n1209\t        // First request used 1 token, so remaining should be 59\n1210\t        expect(remaining1).toBe(59);\n1211\t        // Second request used another token\n1212\t        expect(remaining2).toBe(58);\n1213\t      } finally {\n1214\t        setRateLimiterTestMode(true);\n1215\t        resetRateLimiter();\n1216\t      }\n1217\t    });\n1218\t\n1219\t    it(\"same IP shares rate limit bucket regardless of authentication status\", async () => {\n1220\t      setRateLimiterTestMode(false);\n1221\t      resetRateLimiter();\n1222\t\n1223\t      try {\n1224\t        // Authenticated request from IP 10.0.0.60\n1225\t        const res1 = await requestWithIp(app, \"/api/stations\", \"10.0.0.60\", {\n1226\t          headers: {\n1227\t            Authorization: userCreds.authorizationHeader,\n1228\t            Host: \"mta-my-way.test\",\n1229\t          },\n1230\t        });\n1231\t        const remaining1 = Number(res1.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\") ?? \"60\");\n1232\t\n1233\t        // Unauthenticated request from same IP\n1234\t        const res2 = await requestWithIp(app, \"/api/stations\", \"10.0.0.60\", {\n1235\t          headers: { Host: \"mta-my-way.test\" },\n1236\t        });\n1237\t        const remaining2 = Number(res2.headers.get(\"X-RateLimit-Remaining\") ?? \"60\");\n1238\t\n1239\t        // Both share the same bucket — second request should have fewer remaining\n1240\t        expect(remaining2).toBeLessThan(remaining1);\n1241\t        expect(remaining1).toBe(59);\n1242\t        expect(remaining2).toBe(58);\n1243\t      } finally {\n1244\t        setRateLimiterTestMode(true);\n1245\t        resetRateLimiter();\n1246\t      }\n1247\t    });\n1248\t\n1249\t    it(\"CSRF-blocked requests still consume rate limit tokens\", async () => {\n1250\t      setRateLimiterTestMode(false);\n1251\t      resetRateLimiter();\n1252\t\n1253\t      try {\n1254\t        // POST without CSRF — blocked by CSRF middleware\n1255\t        const res1 = await requestWithIp(app, \"/api/trips\", \"10.0.0.70\", {\n1256\t          method: \"POST\",\n1257\t          headers: {\n1258\t            Authorization: userCreds.authorizationHeader,\n1259\t expect(remaining2).toBeLessThan(remaining1);\n1280\t      } finally {\n1281\t        setRateLimiterTestMode(true);\n1282\t        resetRateLimiter();\n1283\t      }\n1284\t    });\n1285\t\n1286\t    it(\"rate limiter headers show correct limit and reset time\", async () => {\n1287\t      setRateLimiterTestMode(false);\n1288\t      resetRateLimiter();\n1289\t\n1290\t      try {\n1291\t expect(Number(resetAfter)).toBeGreaterThan(0);\n1302\t        }\n1303\t      } finally {\n1304\t        setRateLimiterTestMode(true);\n1305\t        resetRateLimiter();\n1306\t      }\n1307\t    });\n1308\t\n1309\t    it(\"rate limit is independent per endpoint path within same IP\", async () => {\n1310\t      setRateLimiterTestMode(false);\n1311\t      resetRateLimiter();\n1312\t\n1313\t      try {\n1314\t        // Requests to different endpoints from the same IP all share\n1315\t        // one bucket (rate limiter is per-IP, not per-endpoint)\n1316\t        const res1 = await * Integration tests for the full middleware chain.\n *\n * Verifies that middleware execute in the correct order and interact properly:\n *   requestId → securityHeaders → securityLogging → httpMethodRestrictions →\n *   httpRequestSmuggling → httpResponseSplitting → hostHeaderProtection →\n *   tracingMiddleware → requestSizeLimits → inputSanitization →\n *   jsonDepthProtection → optionalAuth → csrfProtection → hppProtection →\n *   httpMetrics → rateLimiter → responseSizeLimits → compressionMiddleware\n *\n * Covers:\n * - Happy-path: authenticated requests to protected endpoints\n * - Failure-path: missing auth, invalid CSRF, rate limits, blocked methods\n * - Security headers present on all responses\n * - Rate limiter counting across middleware\n * - Audit log captures security events from all middleware\n */\n\nimport type {\n  ComplexIndex,\n  RouteIndex,\n  StationIndex,\n  TransferConnection,\n} from \"@mta-my-way/shared\";\nimport type Database from
